5_18_8.NRSVCE: They shall have equal portions to eat, even though they have income from the sale of family possessions.

5_18_9.NKJV: “When you come into the land which the LORD your God is giving you, you shall not learn to follow the abominations of those nations.
5_18_9.KJV: When thou art come into the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee, thou shalt not learn to do after the abominations of those nations.
5_18_9.ESV: “When you come into the land that the LORD your God is giving you, you shall not learn to follow the abominable practices of those nations.
5_18_9.NASB: 'When you enter the land which the LORD your God gives you, you shall not learn to imitate the detestable things of those nations.
5_18_9.NLT: “When you enter the land the LORD your God is giving you, be very careful not to imitate the detestable customs of the nations living there.
5_18_9.WEB: When you have come into the land which Yahweh your God gives you, you shall not learn to imitate the abominations of those nations.
5_18_9.CSB17: “When you enter the land the LORD your God is giving you, do not imitate the detestable customs of those nations.
5_18_9.AMP: "When you enter the land which the LORD your God is giving you, you shall not learn to imitate the detestable (repulsive) practices of those nations.
5_18_9.MSG: When you enter the land that God, your God, is giving you, don't take on the abominable ways of life of the nations there.
5_18_9.NIV: Detestable PracticesWhen you enter the land the Lord your God is giving you, do not learn to imitate the detestable ways of the nations there.
5_18_9.NRSVCE: When you come into the land that the LORD your God is giving you, you must not learn to imitate the abhorrent practices of those nations.

5_18_10.NKJV: There shall not be found among you anyone who makes his son or his daughter pass through the fire, or one who practices witchcraft, or a soothsayer, or one who interprets omens, or a sorcerer,
5_18_10.KJV: There shall not be found among you any one that maketh his son or his daughter to pass through the fire, or that useth divination, or an observer of times, or an enchanter, or a witch,
5_18_10.ESV: There shall not be found among you anyone who burns his son or his daughter as an offering, anyone who practices divination or tells fortunes or interprets omens, or a sorcerer
5_18_10.NASB: 'There shall not be found among you anyone who makes his son or his daughter pass through the fire, one who uses divination, one who practices witchcraft, or one who interprets omens, or a sorcerer,
5_18_10.NLT: For example, never sacrifice your son or daughter as a burnt offering. And do not let your people practice fortune-telling, or use sorcery, or interpret omens, or engage in witchcraft,
5_18_10.WEB: There shall not be found with you anyone who makes his son or his daughter to pass through the fire, one who uses divination, one who practices sorcery, or an enchanter, or a sorcerer,
5_18_10.CSB17: No one among you is to sacrifice his son or daughter in the fire, practice divination, tell fortunes, interpret omens, practice sorcery,
5_18_10.AMP: There shall not be found among you anyone who makes his son or daughter pass through the fire [as a sacrifice], one who uses divination and fortune-telling, one who practices witchcraft, or one who interprets omens, or a sorcerer,
5_18_10.MSG: Don't you dare sacrifice your son or daughter in the fire. Don't practice divination, sorcery, fortunetelling, witchery,
5_18_10.NIV: Let no one be found among you who sacrifices his son or daughter in the fire, who practices divination or sorcery, interprets omens, engages in witchcraft,
5_18_10.NRSVCE: No one shall be found among you who makes a son or daughter pass through fire, or who practices divination, or is a soothsayer, or an augur, or a sorcerer,

5_18_11.NKJV: or one who conjures spells, or a medium, or a spiritist, or one who calls up the dead.
5_18_11.KJV: Or a charmer , or a consulter with familiar spirits, or a wizard, or a necromancer .
5_18_11.ESV: or a charmer or a medium or a necromancer or one who inquires of the dead,
5_18_11.NASB: or one who casts a spell, or a medium, or a spiritist, or one who calls up the dead.
5_18_11.NLT: or cast spells, or function as mediums or psychics, or call forth the spirits of the dead.
5_18_11.WEB: or a charmer, or a consulter with a familiar spirit, or a wizard, or a necromancer.
5_18_11.CSB17: cast spells, consult a medium or a spiritist, or inquire of the dead.
5_18_11.AMP: or one who casts a charm or spell, or a medium, or a spiritist, or a necromancer [who seeks the dead].
5_18_11.MSG: casting spells, holding s-ances, or channeling with the dead.
5_18_11.NIV: or casts spells, or who is a medium or spiritist or who consults the dead.
5_18_11.NRSVCE: or one who casts spells, or who consults ghosts or spirits, or who seeks oracles from the dead.

5_18_12.NKJV: For all who do these things are an abomination to the LORD, and because of these abominations the LORD your God drives them out from before you.
5_18_12.KJV: For all that do these things are an abomination unto the LORD: and because of these abominations the LORD thy God doth drive them out from before thee.
5_18_12.ESV: for whoever does these things is an abomination to the LORD. And because of these abominations the LORD your God is driving them out before you.
5_18_12.NASB: 'For whoever does these things is detestable to the LORD; and because of these detestable things the LORD your God will drive them out before you.
5_18_12.NLT: Anyone who does these things is detestable to the LORD. It is because the other nations have done these detestable things that the LORD your God will drive them out ahead of you.
5_18_12.WEB: For whoever does these things is an abomination to Yahweh. Because of these abominations, Yahweh your God drives them out from before you.
5_18_12.CSB17: Everyone who does these acts is detestable to the LORD, and the LORD your God is driving out the nations before you because of these detestable acts.
5_18_12.AMP: For everyone who does these things is utterly repulsive to the LORD; and because of these detestable practices the LORD your God is driving them out before you.
5_18_12.MSG: People who do these things are an abomination to God. It's because of just such abominable practices that God, your God, is driving these nations out before you.
5_18_12.NIV: Anyone who does these things is detestable to the Lord, and because of these detestable practices the Lord your God will drive out those nations before you.
5_18_12.NRSVCE: For whoever does these things is abhorrent to the LORD; it is because of such abhorrent practices that the LORD your God is driving them out before you.

5_18_13.NKJV: You shall be blameless before the LORD your God.
5_18_13.KJV: Thou shalt be perfect with the LORD thy God. perfect: or, upright, or, sincere
5_18_13.ESV: You shall be blameless before the LORD your God,
5_18_13.NASB: 'You shall be blameless before the LORD your God.
5_18_13.NLT: But you must be blameless before the LORD your God.
5_18_13.WEB: You shall be perfect with Yahweh your God.
5_18_13.CSB17: You must be blameless before the LORD your God.
5_18_13.AMP: You shall be blameless (complete, perfect) before the LORD your God.
5_18_13.MSG: Be completely loyal to God, your God.
5_18_13.NIV: You must be blameless before the Lord your God.
5_18_13.NRSVCE: You must remain completely loyal to the LORD your God.

5_18_14.NKJV: For these nations which you will dispossess listened to soothsayers and diviners; but as for you, the LORD your God has not appointed such for you.
5_18_14.KJV: For these nations, which thou shalt possess, hearkened unto observers of times, and unto diviners: but as for thee, the LORD thy God hath not suffered thee so to do. possess: or, inherit
5_18_14.ESV: for these nations, which you are about to dispossess, listen to fortune-tellers and to diviners. But as for you, the LORD your God has not allowed you to do this.
5_18_14.NASB: 'For those nations, which you shall dispossess, listen to those who practice witchcraft and to diviners, but as for you, the LORD your God has not allowed you [to do] so.
5_18_14.NLT: The nations you are about to displace consult sorcerers and fortune-tellers, but the LORD your God forbids you to do such things.”
5_18_14.WEB: For these nations that you shall dispossess listen to those who practice sorcery and to diviners; but as for you, Yahweh your God has not allowed you so to do.
5_18_14.CSB17: Though these nations you are about to drive out listen to fortune-tellers and diviners, the LORD your God has not permitted you to do this.
5_18_14.AMP: For these nations which you shall dispossess listen to those who practice witchcraft and to diviners and fortune-tellers, but as for you, the LORD your God has not allowed you to do so.
5_18_14.MSG: These nations that you're about to run out of the country consort with sorcerers and witches. But not you. God, your God, forbids it.
5_18_14.NIV: The ProphetThe nations you will dispossess listen to those who practice sorcery or divination. But as for you, the Lord your God has not permitted you to do so.
5_18_14.NRSVCE: Although these nations that you are about to dispossess do give heed to soothsayers and diviners, as for you, the LORD your God does not permit you to do so.

5_18_15.NKJV: “The LORD your God will raise up for you a Prophet like me from your midst, from your brethren. Him you shall hear,
5_18_15.KJV: The LORD thy God will raise up unto thee a Prophet from the midst of thee, of thy brethren, like unto me; unto him ye shall hearken;
5_18_15.ESV: “The LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among you, from your brothers — it is to him you shall listen —
5_18_15.NASB: 'The LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among you, from your countrymen, you shall listen to him.
5_18_15.NLT: Moses continued, “The LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your fellow Israelites. You must listen to him.
5_18_15.WEB: Yahweh your God will raise up to you a prophet from among you, of your brothers, like me. You shall listen to him.
5_18_15.CSB17: “The LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your own brothers. You must listen to him.
5_18_15.AMP: "The LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me [Moses] from among you, from your countrymen (brothers, brethren). You shall listen to him.
5_18_15.MSG: God, your God, is going to raise up a prophet for you. God will raise him up from among your kinsmen, a prophet like me. Listen obediently to him.
5_18_15.NIV: The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your own brothers. You must listen to him.
5_18_15.NRSVCE: The LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your own people; you shall heed such a prophet.

5_18_16.NKJV: according to all you desired of the LORD your God in Horeb in the day of the assembly, saying, ‘Let me not hear again the voice of the LORD my God, nor let me see this great fire anymore, lest I die.’
5_18_16.KJV: According to all that thou desiredst of the LORD thy God in Horeb in the day of the assembly, saying, Let me not hear again the voice of the LORD my God, neither let me see this great fire any more, that I die not.
5_18_16.ESV: just as you desired of the LORD your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly, when you said, ‘Let me not hear again the voice of the LORD my God or see this great fire any more, lest I die.’
5_18_16.NASB: 'This is according to all that you asked of the LORD your God in Horeb on the day of the assembly, saying, 'Let me not hear again the voice of the LORD my God, let me not see this great fire anymore, or I will die.'
5_18_16.NLT: For this is what you yourselves requested of the LORD your God when you were assembled at Mount Sinai. You said, ‘Don’t let us hear the voice of the LORD our God anymore or see this blazing fire, for we will die.’
5_18_16.WEB: This is according to all that you desired of Yahweh your God in Horeb in the day of the assembly, saying, “Let me not hear again Yahweh my God’s voice, neither let me see this great fire any more, that I not die.”
5_18_16.CSB17: This is what you requested from the LORD your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly when you said, ‘Let us not continue to hear the voice of the LORD our God or see this great fire any longer, so that we will not die!’
5_18_16.AMP: This is according to all that you asked of the LORD your God at Horeb (Mount Sinai) on the day of the assembly, saying, 'Let me not hear the voice of the LORD my God again, nor see this great fire anymore, so that I will not die.'
5_18_16.MSG: This is what you asked God, your God, for at Horeb on the day you were all gathered at the mountain and said, "We can't hear any more from God, our God; we can't stand seeing any more fire. We'll die!"
5_18_16.NIV: For this is what you asked of the Lord your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly when you said, “Let us not hear the voice of the Lord our God nor see this great fire anymore, or we will die.”
5_18_16.NRSVCE: This is what you requested of the LORD your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly when you said: “If I hear the voice of the LORD my God any more, or ever again see this great fire, I will die.”

5_18_17.NKJV: “And the LORD said to me: ‘What they have spoken is good.
5_18_17.KJV: And the LORD said unto me, They have well spoken that which they have spoken.
5_18_17.ESV: And the LORD said to me, ‘They are right in what they have spoken.
5_18_17.NASB: 'The LORD said to me, 'They have spoken well.
5_18_17.NLT: “Then the LORD said to me, ‘What they have said is right.
5_18_17.WEB: Yahweh said to me, “They have well said that which they have spoken.
5_18_17.CSB17: Then the LORD said to me, ‘They have spoken well.
5_18_17.AMP: The LORD said to me, 'They have spoken well.
5_18_17.MSG: And God said to me, "They're right; they've spoken the truth.
5_18_17.NIV: The Lord said to me: “What they say is good.
5_18_17.NRSVCE: Then the LORD replied to me: “They are right in what they have said.

5_18_18.NKJV: I will raise up for them a Prophet like you from among their brethren, and will put My words in His mouth, and He shall speak to them all that I command Him.
5_18_18.KJV: I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put my words in his mouth; and he shall speak unto them all that I shall command him.
5_18_18.ESV: I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their brothers. And I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him.
5_18_18.NASB: 'I will raise up a prophet from among their countrymen like you, and I will put My words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him.
5_18_18.NLT: I will raise up a prophet like you from among their fellow Israelites. I will put my words in his mouth, and he will tell the people everything I command him.
5_18_18.WEB: I will raise them up a prophet from among their brothers, like you. I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I shall command him.
5_18_18.CSB17: I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their brothers. I will put my words in his mouth, and he will tell them everything I command him.
5_18_18.AMP: I will raise up a prophet from among their countrymen like you, and I will put My words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him.
5_18_18.MSG: I'll raise up for them a prophet like you from their kinsmen. I'll tell him what to say and he will pass on to them everything I command him.
5_18_18.NIV: I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their brothers; I will put my words in his mouth, and he will tell them everything I command him.
5_18_18.NRSVCE: I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their own people; I will put my words in the mouth of the prophet, who shall speak to them everything that I command.

5_18_19.NKJV: And it shall be that whoever will not hear My words, which He speaks in My name, I will require it of him.
5_18_19.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever will not hearken unto my words which he shall speak in my name, I will require it of him.
5_18_19.ESV: And whoever will not listen to my words that he shall speak in my name, I myself will require it of him.
5_18_19.NASB: 'It shall come about that whoever will not listen to My words which he shall speak in My name, I Myself will require [it] of him.
5_18_19.NLT: I will personally deal with anyone who will not listen to the messages the prophet proclaims on my behalf.
5_18_19.WEB: It shall happen, that whoever will not listen to my words which he shall speak in my name, I will require it of him.
5_18_19.CSB17: I will hold accountable whoever does not listen to my words that he speaks in my name.
5_18_19.AMP: It shall come about that whoever will not listen to My words which he shall speak in My name, I Myself will require it of him [and there will be consequences].
5_18_19.MSG: And anyone who won't listen to my words spoken by him, I will personally hold responsible.
5_18_19.NIV: If anyone does not listen to my words that the prophet speaks in my name, I myself will call him to account.
5_18_19.NRSVCE: Anyone who does not heed the words that the prophet shall speak in my name, I myself will hold accountable.

5_18_20.NKJV: But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in My name, which I have not commanded him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other gods, that prophet shall die.’
5_18_20.KJV: But the prophet, which shall presume to speak a word in my name, which I have not commanded him to speak, or that shall speak in the name of other gods, even that prophet shall die.
5_18_20.ESV: But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in my name that I have not commanded him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other gods, that same prophet shall die.’
5_18_20.NASB: 'But the prophet who speaks a word presumptuously in My name which I have not commanded him to speak, or which he speaks in the name of other gods, that prophet shall die.'
5_18_20.NLT: But any prophet who falsely claims to speak in my name or who speaks in the name of another god must die.’
5_18_20.WEB: But the prophet who speaks a word presumptuously in my name, which I have not commanded him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other gods, that same prophet shall die.”
5_18_20.CSB17: But the prophet who presumes to speak a message in my name that I have not commanded him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other gods — that prophet must die.’
5_18_20.AMP: But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in My name which I have not commanded him to speak, or which he speaks in the name of other gods—that prophet shall die.'
5_18_20.MSG: "But any prophet who fakes it, who claims to speak in my name something I haven't commanded him to say, or speaks in the name of other gods, that prophet must die."
5_18_20.NIV: But a prophet who presumes to speak in my name anything I have not commanded him to say, or a prophet who speaks in the name of other gods, must be put to death.”
5_18_20.NRSVCE: But any prophet who speaks in the name of other gods, or who presumes to speak in my name a word that I have not commanded the prophet to speak — that prophet shall die.”

5_18_21.NKJV: And if you say in your heart, ‘How shall we know the word which the LORD has not spoken?’—
5_18_21.KJV: And if thou say in thine heart, How shall we know the word which the LORD hath not spoken?
5_18_21.ESV: And if you say in your heart, ‘How may we know the word that the LORD has not spoken?’ —
5_18_21.NASB: 'You may say in your heart, 'How will we know the word which the LORD has not spoken?'
5_18_21.NLT: “But you may wonder, ‘How will we know whether or not a prophecy is from the LORD?’
5_18_21.WEB: You may say in your heart, “How shall we know the word which Yahweh has not spoken?”
5_18_21.CSB17: You may say to yourself, ‘How can we recognize a message the LORD has not spoken?’
5_18_21.AMP: If you say in your heart, 'How will we know and recognize the word which the LORD has not spoken?'
5_18_21.MSG: You may be wondering among yourselves, "How can we tell the difference, whether it was God who spoke or not?" Here's how:
5_18_21.NIV: You may say to yourselves, “How can we know when a message has not been spoken by the Lord?”
5_18_21.NRSVCE: You may say to yourself, “How can we recognize a word that the LORD has not spoken?”

5_18_22.NKJV: when a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD, if the thing does not happen or come to pass, that is the thing which the LORD has not spoken; the prophet has spoken it presumptuously; you shall not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.KJV: When a prophet speaketh in the name of the LORD, if the thing follow not, nor come to pass, that is the thing which the LORD hath not spoken, but the prophet hath spoken it presumptuously: thou shalt not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.ESV: when a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD, if the word does not come to pass or come true, that is a word that the LORD has not spoken; the prophet has spoken it presumptuously. You need not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.NASB: 'When a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD, if the thing does not come about or come true, that is the thing which the LORD has not spoken. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously; you shall not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.NLT: If the prophet speaks in the LORD’s name but his prediction does not happen or come true, you will know that the LORD did not give that message. That prophet has spoken without my authority and need not be feared.
5_18_22.WEB: When a prophet speaks in Yahweh’s name, if the thing doesn’t follow, nor happen, that is the thing which Yahweh has not spoken. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously. You shall not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.CSB17: When a prophet speaks in the LORD’s name, and the message does not come true or is not fulfilled, that is a message the LORD has not spoken. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously. Do not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.AMP: When a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD and the thing does not happen or come true, that is the thing which the LORD has not spoken. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously; you shall not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.MSG: If what the prophet spoke in God's name doesn't happen, then obviously God wasn't behind it; the prophet made it up. Forget about him.
5_18_22.NIV: If what a prophet proclaims in the name of the Lord does not take place or come true, that is a message the Lord has not spoken. That prophet has spoken presumptuously. Do not be afraid of him.
5_18_22.NRSVCE: If a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD but the thing does not take place or prove true, it is a word that the LORD has not spoken. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously; do not be frightened by it.

5_19_1.NKJV: “When the LORD your God has cut off the nations whose land the LORD your God is giving you, and you dispossess them and dwell in their cities and in their houses,
5_19_1.KJV: When the LORD thy God hath cut off the nations, whose land the LORD thy God giveth thee, and thou succeedest them, and dwellest in their cities, and in their houses; succeedest: Heb. inheritest, or, possessest
5_19_1.ESV: “When the LORD your God cuts off the nations whose land the LORD your God is giving you, and you dispossess them and dwell in their cities and in their houses,
5_19_1.NASB: 'When the LORD your God cuts off the nations, whose land the LORD your God gives you, and you dispossess them and settle in their cities and in their houses,
5_19_1.NLT: “When the LORD your God destroys the nations whose land he is giving you, you will take over their land and settle in their towns and homes.
5_19_1.WEB: When Yahweh your God cuts off the nations, whose land Yahweh your God gives you, and you succeed them, and dwell in their cities, and in their houses;
5_19_1.CSB17: “When the LORD your God annihilates the nations whose land he is giving you, so that you drive them out and live in their cities and houses,
5_19_1.AMP: "When the LORD your God cuts off (destroys) the nations whose land He is giving you, and you dispossess them and live in their cities and in their houses,
5_19_1.MSG: When God, your God, throws the nations out of the country that God, your God, is giving you and you settle down in their cities and houses,
5_19_1.NIV: Cities of RefugeWhen the Lord your God has destroyed the nations whose land he is giving you, and when you have driven them out and settled in their towns and houses,
5_19_1.NRSVCE: When the LORD your God has cut off the nations whose land the LORD your God is giving you, and you have dispossessed them and settled in their towns and in their houses,

5_19_2.NKJV: you shall separate three cities for yourself in the midst of your land which the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_2.KJV: Thou shalt separate three cities for thee in the midst of thy land, which the LORD thy God giveth thee to possess it.
5_19_2.ESV: you shall set apart three cities for yourselves in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_2.NASB: you shall set aside three cities for yourself in the midst of your land, which the LORD your God gives you to possess.
5_19_2.NLT: Then you must set apart three cities of refuge in the land the LORD your God is giving you.
5_19_2.WEB: you shall set apart three cities for yourselves in the middle of your land, which Yahweh your God gives you to possess it.
5_19_2.CSB17: you are to set apart three cities for yourselves within the land the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_2.AMP: you shall designate three cities for yourself in the central area of the land, which the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_2.MSG: you are to set aside three easily accessible cities in the land that God, your God, is giving you as your very own.
5_19_2.NIV: then set aside for yourselves three cities centrally located in the land the Lord your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_2.NRSVCE: you shall set apart three cities in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.

5_19_3.NKJV: You shall prepare roads for yourself, and divide into three parts the territory of your land which the LORD your God is giving you to inherit, that any manslayer may flee there.
5_19_3.KJV: Thou shalt prepare thee a way, and divide the coasts of thy land, which the LORD thy God giveth thee to inherit, into three parts, that every slayer may flee thither.
5_19_3.ESV: You shall measure the distances and divide into three parts the area of the land that the LORD your God gives you as a possession, so that any manslayer can flee to them.
5_19_3.NASB: 'You shall prepare the roads for yourself, and divide into three parts the territory of your land which the LORD your God will give you as a possession, so that any manslayer may flee there.
5_19_3.NLT: Survey the territory, and divide the land the LORD your God is giving you into three districts, with one of these cities in each district. Then anyone who has killed someone can flee to one of the cities of refuge for safety.
5_19_3.WEB: You shall prepare the way, and divide the borders of your land, which Yahweh your God causes you to inherit, into three parts, that every man slayer may flee there.
5_19_3.CSB17: You are to determine the distances and divide the land the LORD your God is granting you as an inheritance into three regions, so that anyone who commits manslaughter can flee to these cities.
5_19_3.AMP: You shall prepare and maintain for yourself the roads [to these cities], and divide the territory of your land into three parts, so that anyone who kills another unintentionally may escape there [for asylum].
5_19_3.MSG: Divide your land into thirds, this land that God, your God, is giving you to possess, and build roads to the towns so that anyone who accidentally kills another can flee there.
5_19_3.NIV: Build roads to them and divide into three parts the land the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance, so that anyone who kills a man may flee there.
5_19_3.NRSVCE: You shall calculate the distances and divide into three regions the land that the LORD your God gives you as a possession, so that any homicide can flee to one of them.

5_19_4.NKJV: “And this is the case of the manslayer who flees there, that he may live: Whoever kills his neighbor unintentionally, not having hated him in time past—
5_19_4.KJV: And this is the case of the slayer, which shall flee thither, that he may live: Whoso killeth his neighbour ignorantly , whom he hated not in time past; in: Heb. from yesterday the third day
5_19_4.ESV: “This is the provision for the manslayer, who by fleeing there may save his life. If anyone kills his neighbor unintentionally without having hated him in the past —
5_19_4.NASB: 'Now this is the case of the manslayer who may flee there and live: when he kills his friend unintentionally, not hating him previously--
5_19_4.NLT: “If someone kills another person unintentionally, without previous hostility, the slayer may flee to any of these cities to live in safety.
5_19_4.WEB: This is the case of the man slayer who shall flee there and live. Whoever kills his neighbor unawares, and didn’t hate him in time past;
5_19_4.CSB17: “Here is the law concerning a case of someone who kills a person and flees there to save his life, having killed his neighbor accidentally without previously hating him:
5_19_4.AMP: "Now this is the case of the offender (manslayer) who may escape there and live [protected from vengeance]: when he kills his neighbor unintentionally, not having hated him previously—
5_19_4.MSG: This is the guideline for the murderer who flees there to take refuge: He has to have killed his neighbor without premeditation and with no history of bad blood between them.
5_19_4.NIV: This is the rule concerning the man who kills another and flees there to save his life—one who kills his neighbor unintentionally, without malice aforethought.
5_19_4.NRSVCE: Now this is the case of a homicide who might flee there and live, that is, someone who has killed another person unintentionally when the two had not been at enmity before:

5_19_5.NKJV: as when a man goes to the woods with his neighbor to cut timber, and his hand swings a stroke with the ax to cut down the tree, and the head slips from the handle and strikes his neighbor so that he dies—he shall flee to one of these cities and live;
5_19_5.KJV: As when a man goeth into the wood with his neighbour to hew wood, and his hand fetcheth a stroke with the axe to cut down the tree, and the head slippeth from the helve, and lighteth upon his neighbour, that he die; he shall flee unto one of those cities, and live: head: Heb. iron helve: Heb. wood lighteth: Heb. findeth
5_19_5.ESV: as when someone goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood, and his hand swings the axe to cut down a tree, and the head slips from the handle and strikes his neighbor so that he dies — he may flee to one of these cities and live,
5_19_5.NASB: as when [a man] goes into the forest with his friend to cut wood, and his hand swings the axe to cut down the tree, and the iron [head] slips off the handle and strikes his friend so that he dies-- he may flee to one of these cities and live;
5_19_5.NLT: For example, suppose someone goes into the forest with a neighbor to cut wood. And suppose one of them swings an ax to chop down a tree, and the ax head flies off the handle, killing the other person. In such cases, the slayer may flee to one of the cities of refuge to live in safety.
5_19_5.WEB: as when a man goes into the forest with his neighbor to chop wood, and his hand fetches a stroke with the ax to cut down the tree, and the head slips from the handle, and lights on his neighbor, so that he dies, he shall flee to one of these cities and live.
5_19_5.CSB17: If, for example, he goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut timber, and his hand swings the ax to chop down a tree, but the blade flies off the handle and strikes his neighbor so that he dies, that person may flee to one of these cities and live.
5_19_5.AMP: as [for example] when a man goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood, and his hand swings the axe to cut down the tree, but the iron head slips off the wooden handle and hits his companion and he dies—the offender may escape to one of these cities and live;
5_19_5.MSG: For instance, a man goes with his neighbor into the woods to cut a tree; he swings the ax, the head slips off the handle and hits his neighbor, killing him. He may then flee to one of these cities and save his life.
5_19_5.NIV: For instance, a man may go into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood, and as he swings his ax to fell a tree, the head may fly off and hit his neighbor and kill him. That man may flee to one of these cities and save his life.
5_19_5.NRSVCE: Suppose someone goes into the forest with another to cut wood, and when one of them swings the ax to cut down a tree, the head slips from the handle and strikes the other person who then dies; the killer may flee to one of these cities and live.

5_19_6.NKJV: lest the avenger of blood, while his anger is hot, pursue the manslayer and overtake him, because the way is long, and kill him, though he was not deserving of death, since he had not hated the victim in time past.
5_19_6.KJV: Lest the avenger of the blood pursue the slayer , while his heart is hot, and overtake him, because the way is long, and slay him; whereas he was not worthy of death, inasmuch as he hated him not in time past. slay: Heb. smite him in life in: Heb. from yesterday the third day
5_19_6.ESV: lest the avenger of blood in hot anger pursue the manslayer and overtake him, because the way is long, and strike him fatally, though the man did not deserve to die, since he had not hated his neighbor in the past.
5_19_6.NASB: otherwise the avenger of blood might pursue the manslayer in the heat of his anger, and overtake him, because the way is long, and take his life, though he was not deserving of death, since he had not hated him previously.
5_19_6.NLT: “If the distance to the nearest city of refuge is too far, an enraged avenger might be able to chase down and kill the person who caused the death. Then the slayer would die unfairly, since he had never shown hostility toward the person who died.
5_19_6.WEB: Otherwise, the avenger of blood might pursue the man slayer, while his heart is hot, and overtake him, because the way is long, and strike him mortally; even though he was not worthy of death, because he didn’t hate him in time past.
5_19_6.CSB17: Otherwise, the avenger of blood in the heat of his anger might pursue the one who committed manslaughter, overtake him because the distance is great, and strike him dead. Yet he did not deserve to die, since he did not previously hate his neighbor.
5_19_6.AMP: otherwise the avenger of blood might pursue the offender in the heat of anger, and overtake him, because it is a long way, and take his life, even though he did not deserve to die, since he did not hate his neighbor beforehand.
5_19_6.MSG: If the city is too far away, the avenger of blood racing in hot-blooded pursuit might catch him since it's such a long distance, and kill him even though he didn't deserve it. It wasn't his fault. There was no history of hatred between them.
5_19_6.NIV: Otherwise, the avenger of blood might pursue him in a rage, overtake him if the distance is too great, and kill him even though he is not deserving of death, since he did it to his neighbor without malice aforethought.
5_19_6.NRSVCE: But if the distance is too great, the avenger of blood in hot anger might pursue and overtake and put the killer to death, although a death sentence was not deserved, since the two had not been at enmity before.

5_19_7.NRSVCE: Therefore I command you: You shall set apart three cities.

5_19_10.NKJV: lest innocent blood be shed in the midst of your land which the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, and thus guilt of bloodshed be upon you.
5_19_10.KJV: That innocent blood be not shed in thy land, which the LORD thy God giveth thee for an inheritance, and so blood be upon thee.
5_19_10.ESV: lest innocent blood be shed in your land that the LORD your God is giving you for an inheritance, and so the guilt of bloodshed be upon you.
5_19_10.NASB: 'So innocent blood will not be shed in the midst of your land which the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance, and bloodguiltiness be on you.
5_19_10.NLT: That way you will prevent the death of innocent people in the land the LORD your God is giving you as your special possession. You will not be held responsible for the death of innocent people.
5_19_10.WEB: This is so that innocent blood will not be shed in the middle of your land which Yahweh your God gives you for an inheritance, leaving blood guilt on you.
5_19_10.CSB17: In this way, innocent blood will not be shed, and you will not become guilty of bloodshed in the land the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance.
5_19_10.AMP: so that innocent blood will not be shed [by blood avengers] in your land which the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, and blood guilt will not be on you [for the death of an innocent man].
5_19_10.MSG: so that there is no chance of innocent blood being spilled in your land. God, your God, is giving you this land as an inheritance—you don't want to pollute it with innocent blood and bring bloodguilt upon yourselves.
5_19_10.NIV: Do this so that innocent blood will not be shed in your land, which the Lord your God is giving you as your inheritance, and so that you will not be guilty of bloodshed.
5_19_10.NRSVCE: so that the blood of an innocent person may not be shed in the land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, thereby bringing bloodguilt upon you.

5_19_11.NKJV: “But if anyone hates his neighbor, lies in wait for him, rises against him and strikes him mortally, so that he dies, and he flees to one of these cities,
5_19_11.KJV: But if any man hate his neighbour, and lie in wait for him, and rise up against him, and smite him mortally that he die, and fleeth into one of these cities: mortally: Heb. in life
5_19_11.ESV: “But if anyone hates his neighbor and lies in wait for him and attacks him and strikes him fatally so that he dies, and he flees into one of these cities,
5_19_11.NASB: 'But if there is a man who hates his neighbor and lies in wait for him and rises up against him and strikes him so that he dies, and he flees to one of these cities,

5_19_11.WEB: But if any man hates his neighbor, lies in wait for him, rises up against him, strikes him mortally so that he dies, and he flees into one of these cities;
5_19_11.CSB17: But if someone hates his neighbor, lies in ambush for him, attacks him, and strikes him fatally, and flees to one of these cities,

5_19_12.NKJV: then the elders of his city shall send and bring him from there, and deliver him over to the hand of the avenger of blood, that he may die.
5_19_12.KJV: Then the elders of his city shall send and fetch him thence, and deliver him into the hand of the avenger of blood, that he may die.

5_19_12.NASB: then the elders of his city shall send and take him from there and deliver him into the hand of the avenger of blood, that he may die.

5_19_12.WEB: then the elders of his city shall send and bring him there, and deliver him into the hand of the avenger of blood, that he may die.
5_19_12.CSB17: the elders of his city are to send for him, take him from there, and hand him over to the avenger of blood and he will die.
5_19_12.AMP: then the elders of his own city shall send for him and have him taken back from there and turn him over to the avenger of blood, so that he may be put to death.

5_19_12.NIV: the elders of his town shall send for him, bring him back from the city, and hand him over to the avenger of blood to die.
5_19_12.NRSVCE: then the elders of the killer’s city shall send to have the culprit taken from there and handed over to the avenger of blood to be put to death.

5_19_13.NKJV: Your eye shall not pity him, but you shall put away the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, that it may go well with you.
5_19_13.KJV: Thine eye shall not pity him, but thou shalt put away the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, that it may go well with thee.
5_19_13.ESV: Your eye shall not pity him, but you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, so that it may be well with you.
5_19_13.NASB: 'You shall not pity him, but you shall purge the blood of the innocent from Israel, that it may go well with you.
5_19_13.NLT: Do not feel sorry for that murderer! Purge from Israel the guilt of murdering innocent people; then all will go well with you.
5_19_13.WEB: Your eye shall not pity him, but you shall purge the innocent blood from Israel, that it may go well with you.
5_19_13.CSB17: Do not look on him with pity but purge from Israel the guilt of shedding innocent blood, and you will prosper.
5_19_13.AMP: You shall not pity him [the guilty one], but you shall purge the blood of the innocent from Israel, so that it may go well with you.
5_19_13.MSG: Don't feel sorry for him. Clean out the pollution of wrongful murder from Israel so that you'll be able to live well and breathe clean air.
5_19_13.NIV: Show him no pity. You must purge from Israel the guilt of shedding innocent blood, so that it may go well with you.
5_19_13.NRSVCE: Show no pity; you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, so that it may go well with you.

5_19_14.NKJV: “You shall not remove your neighbor’s landmark, which the men of old have set, in your inheritance which you will inherit in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_14.KJV: Thou shalt not remove thy neighbour's landmark, which they of old time have set in thine inheritance, which thou shalt inherit in the land that the LORD thy God giveth thee to possess it.
5_19_14.ESV: “You shall not move your neighbor's landmark, which the men of old have set, in the inheritance that you will hold in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_14.NASB: 'You shall not move your neighbor's boundary mark, which the ancestors have set, in your inheritance which you will inherit in the land that the LORD your God gives you to possess.
5_19_14.NLT: “When you arrive in the land the LORD your God is giving you as your special possession, you must never steal anyone’s land by moving the boundary markers your ancestors set up to mark their property.
5_19_14.WEB: You shall not remove your neighbor’s landmark, which they of old time have set, in your inheritance which you shall inherit, in the land that Yahweh your God gives you to possess.
5_19_14.CSB17: “Do not move your neighbor’s boundary marker, established at the start in the inheritance you will receive in the land the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_14.AMP: "You shall not move your neighbor's boundary mark, which the forefathers [who first divided the territory] have set, in the land which you will inherit in the land which the LORD your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_14.MSG: Don't move your neighbor's boundary markers, the longstanding landmarks set up by your pioneer ancestors defining their property.
5_19_14.NIV: Do not move your neighbor’s boundary stone set up by your predecessors in the inheritance you receive in the land the Lord your God is giving you to possess.
5_19_14.NRSVCE: You must not move your neighbor’s boundary marker, set up by former generations, on the property that will be allotted to you in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.

5_19_15.NKJV: “One witness shall not rise against a man concerning any iniquity or any sin that he commits; by the mouth of two or three witnesses the matter shall be established.
5_19_15.KJV: One witness shall not rise up against a man for any iniquity, or for any sin, in any sin that he sinneth: at the mouth of two witnesses, or at the mouth of three witnesses, shall the matter be established.
5_19_15.ESV: “A single witness shall not suffice against a person for any crime or for any wrong in connection with any offense that he has committed. Only on the evidence of two witnesses or of three witnesses shall a charge be established.
5_19_15.NASB: 'A single witness shall not rise up against a man on account of any iniquity or any sin which he has committed; on the evidence of two or three witnesses a matter shall be confirmed.
5_19_15.NLT: “You must not convict anyone of a crime on the testimony of only one witness. The facts of the case must be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses.
5_19_15.WEB: One witness shall not rise up against a man for any iniquity, or for any sin, in any sin that he sins. At the mouth of two witnesses, or at the mouth of three witnesses, shall a matter be established.
5_19_15.CSB17: “One witness cannot establish any iniquity or sin against a person, whatever that person has done. A fact must be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses.
5_19_15.AMP: "A single witness shall not appear in a trial against a man for any wrong or any sin which he has committed; [only] on the testimony or evidence of two or three witnesses shall a charge be confirmed.
5_19_15.MSG: You cannot convict anyone of a crime or sin on the word of one witness. You need two or three witnesses to make a case.
5_19_15.NIV: WitnessesOne witness is not enough to convict a man accused of any crime or offense he may have committed. A matter must be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses.
5_19_15.NRSVCE: A single witness shall not suffice to convict a person of any crime or wrongdoing in connection with any offense that may be committed. Only on the evidence of two or three witnesses shall a charge be sustained.

5_19_17.NKJV: then both men in the controversy shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who serve in those days.
5_19_17.KJV: Then both the men, between whom the controversy is, shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges, which shall be in those days;
5_19_17.ESV: then both parties to the dispute shall appear before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who are in office in those days.
5_19_17.NASB: then both the men who have the dispute shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who will be [in office] in those days.

5_19_17.WEB: then both the men, between whom the controversy is, shall stand before Yahweh, before the priests and the judges who shall be in those days;
5_19_17.CSB17: the two people in the dispute are to stand in the presence of the LORD before the priests and judges in authority at that time.
5_19_17.AMP: then both parties to the controversy shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who will be in office at that time.
5_19_17.MSG: then both parties involved in the quarrel must stand in the Presence of God before the priests and judges who are in office at that time.
5_19_17.NIV: the two men involved in the dispute must stand in the presence of the Lord before the priests and the judges who are in office at the time.
5_19_17.NRSVCE: then both parties to the dispute shall appear before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who are in office in those days,

5_19_18.NKJV: And the judges shall make careful inquiry, and indeed, if the witness is a false witness, who has testified falsely against his brother,
5_19_18.KJV: And the judges shall make diligent inquisition: and, behold, if the witness be a false witness, and hath testified falsely against his brother;
5_19_18.ESV: The judges shall inquire diligently, and if the witness is a false witness and has accused his brother falsely,
5_19_18.NASB: 'The judges shall investigate thoroughly, and if the witness is a false witness [and] he has accused his brother falsely,
5_19_18.NLT: The judges must investigate the case thoroughly. If the accuser has brought false charges against his fellow Israelite,
5_19_18.WEB: and the judges shall make diligent inquisition: and, behold, if the witness is a false witness, and has testified falsely against his brother;
5_19_18.CSB17: The judges are to make a careful investigation, and if the witness turns out to be a liar who has falsely accused his brother,
5_19_18.AMP: The judges shall investigate thoroughly, and if the witness is a false witness, and he has accused his brother falsely,
5_19_18.MSG: The judges must conduct a careful investigation; if the witness turns out to be a false witness and has lied against his fellow Israelite,
5_19_18.NIV: The judges must make a thorough investigation, and if the witness proves to be a liar, giving false testimony against his brother,
5_19_18.NRSVCE: and the judges shall make a thorough inquiry. If the witness is a false witness, having testified falsely against another,

5_19_19.NKJV: then you shall do to him as he thought to have done to his brother; so you shall put away the evil from among you.
5_19_19.KJV: Then shall ye do unto him, as he had thought to have done unto his brother: so shalt thou put the evil away from among you.
5_19_19.ESV: then you shall do to him as he had meant to do to his brother. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.
5_19_19.NASB: then you shall do to him just as he had intended to do to his brother. Thus you shall purge the evil from among you.
5_19_19.NLT: you must impose on the accuser the sentence he intended for the other person. In this way, you will purge such evil from among you.
5_19_19.WEB: then you shall do to him as he had thought to do to his brother. So you shall remove the evil from among you.
5_19_19.CSB17: you must do to him as he intended to do to his brother. You must purge the evil from you.
5_19_19.AMP: then you shall do to him just as he had intended to do to his brother. So you shall remove the evil from among you.
5_19_19.MSG: give him the same medicine he intended for the other party. Clean the polluting evil from your company.
5_19_19.NIV: then do to him as he intended to do to his brother. You must purge the evil from among you.
5_19_19.NRSVCE: then you shall do to the false witness just as the false witness had meant to do to the other. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

5_19_20.NKJV: And those who remain shall hear and fear, and hereafter they shall not again commit such evil among you.
5_19_20.KJV: And those which remain shall hear, and fear, and shall henceforth commit no more any such evil among you.
5_19_20.ESV: And the rest shall hear and fear, and shall never again commit any such evil among you.
5_19_20.NASB: 'The rest will hear and be afraid, and will never again do such an evil thing among you.
5_19_20.NLT: Then the rest of the people will hear about it and be afraid to do such an evil thing.
5_19_20.WEB: Those who remain shall hear, and fear, and will never again commit any such evil among you.
5_19_20.CSB17: Then everyone else will hear and be afraid, and they will never again do anything evil like this among you.
5_19_20.AMP: Those who remain will hear and be afraid, and will never again do such an evil thing among you.
5_19_20.MSG: People will hear of what you've done and be impressed; that will put a stop to this kind of evil among you.
5_19_20.NIV: The rest of the people will hear of this and be afraid, and never again will such an evil thing be done among you.
5_19_20.NRSVCE: The rest shall hear and be afraid, and a crime such as this shall never again be committed among you.

5_19_21.NKJV: Your eye shall not pity: life shall be for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.KJV: And thine eye shall not pity; but life shall go for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.ESV: Your eye shall not pity. It shall be life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.NASB: 'Thus you shall not show pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.NLT: You must show no pity for the guilty! Your rule should be life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.WEB: Your eyes shall not pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.CSB17: Do not show pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, and foot for foot.
5_19_21.AMP: You shall not show pity [to the guilty one]: it shall be life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.MSG: Don't feel sorry for the person: It's life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.NIV: Show no pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
5_19_21.NRSVCE: Show no pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

5_20_1.NKJV: “When you go out to battle against your enemies, and see horses and chariots and people more numerous than you, do not be afraid of them; for the LORD your God is with you, who brought you up from the land of Egypt.
5_20_1.KJV: When thou goest out to battle against thine enemies, and seest horses, and chariots, and a people more than thou, be not afraid of them: for the LORD thy God is with thee, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
5_20_1.ESV: “When you go out to war against your enemies, and see horses and chariots and an army larger than your own, you shall not be afraid of them, for the LORD your God is with you, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.
5_20_1.NASB: 'When you go out to battle against your enemies and see horses and chariots [and] people more numerous than you, do not be afraid of them; for the LORD your God, who brought you up from the land of Egypt, is with you.
5_20_1.NLT: “When you go out to fight your enemies and you face horses and chariots and an army greater than your own, do not be afraid. The LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, is with you!
5_20_1.WEB: When you go out to battle against your enemies, and see horses, chariots, and a people more than you, you shall not be afraid of them; for Yahweh your God is with you, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.
5_20_1.CSB17: “When you go out to war against your enemies and see horses, chariots, and an army larger than yours, do not be afraid of them, for the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, is with you.
5_20_1.AMP: "When you go out to battle against your enemies and see horses and chariots and people more numerous than you, do not be afraid of them; for the LORD your God, who brought you up from the land of Egypt, is with you.
5_20_1.MSG: When you go to war against your enemy and see horses and chariots and soldiers far outnumbering you, do not recoil in fear of them; God, your God, who brought you up out of Egypt is with you.
5_20_1.NIV: Going to WarWhen you go to war against your enemies and see horses and chariots and an army greater than yours, do not be afraid of them, because the Lord your God, who brought you up out of Egypt, will be with you.
5_20_1.NRSVCE: When you go out to war against your enemies, and see horses and chariots, an army larger than your own, you shall not be afraid of them; for the LORD your God is with you, who brought you up from the land of Egypt.

5_20_2.NKJV: So it shall be, when you are on the verge of battle, that the priest shall approach and speak to the people.
5_20_2.KJV: And it shall be, when ye are come nigh unto the battle, that the priest shall approach and speak unto the people,
5_20_2.ESV: And when you draw near to the battle, the priest shall come forward and speak to the people
5_20_2.NASB: 'When you are approaching the battle, the priest shall come near and speak to the people.
5_20_2.NLT: When you prepare for battle, the priest must come forward to speak to the troops.
5_20_2.WEB: It shall be, when you draw near to the battle, that the priest shall approach and speak to the people,
5_20_2.CSB17: When you are about to engage in battle, the priest is to come forward and address the army.
5_20_2.AMP: When you approach the battle, the priest shall come forward and speak to the people,
5_20_2.MSG: When the battle is about to begin, let the priest come forward and speak to the troops.
5_20_2.NIV: When you are about to go into battle, the priest shall come forward and address the army.
5_20_2.NRSVCE: Before you engage in battle, the priest shall come forward and speak to the troops,

5_20_3.NKJV: And he shall say to them, ‘Hear, O Israel: Today you are on the verge of battle with your enemies. Do not let your heart faint, do not be afraid, and do not tremble or be terrified because of them;
5_20_3.KJV: And shall say unto them, Hear, O Israel, ye approach this day unto battle against your enemies: let not your hearts faint, fear not, and do not tremble, neither be ye terrified because of them; faint: Heb. be tender tremble: Heb. make haste
5_20_3.ESV: and shall say to them, ‘Hear, O Israel, today you are drawing near for battle against your enemies: let not your heart faint. Do not fear or panic or be in dread of them,
5_20_3.NASB: 'He shall say to them, 'Hear, O Israel, you are approaching the battle against your enemies today. Do not be fainthearted. Do not be afraid, or panic, or tremble before them,
5_20_3.NLT: He will say to them, ‘Listen to me, all you men of Israel! Do not be afraid as you go out to fight your enemies today! Do not lose heart or panic or tremble before them.
5_20_3.WEB: and shall tell them, “Hear, Israel, you draw near today to battle against your enemies. Don’t let your heart faint! Don’t be afraid, nor tremble, neither be scared of them;
5_20_3.CSB17: He is to say to them: ‘Listen, Israel: Today you are about to engage in battle with your enemies. Do not be cowardly. Do not be afraid, alarmed, or terrified because of them.
5_20_3.AMP: and shall say to them, 'Hear, O Israel: you are advancing today to battle against your enemies. Do not lack courage. Do not be afraid, or panic, or tremble [in terror] before them,
5_20_3.MSG: He'll say, "Attention, Israel. In a few minutes you're going to do battle with your enemies. Don't waver in resolve. Don't fear. Don't hesitate. Don't panic.
5_20_3.NIV: He shall say: “Hear, O Israel, today you are going into battle against your enemies. Do not be fainthearted or afraid; do not be terrified or give way to panic before them.
5_20_3.NRSVCE: and shall say to them: “Hear, O Israel! Today you are drawing near to do battle against your enemies. Do not lose heart, or be afraid, or panic, or be in dread of them;

5_20_4.NKJV: for the LORD your God is He who goes with you, to fight for you against your enemies, to save you.’
5_20_4.KJV: For the LORD your God is he that goeth with you, to fight for you against your enemies, to save you.
5_20_4.ESV: for the LORD your God is he who goes with you to fight for you against your enemies, to give you the victory.’
5_20_4.NASB: for the LORD your God is the one who goes with you, to fight for you against your enemies, to save you.'
