5_23_18.NIV: You must not bring the earnings of a female prostitute or of a male prostitute into the house of the Lord your God to pay any vow, because the Lord your God detests them both.
5_23_18.NRSVCE: You shall not bring the fee of a prostitute or the wages of a male prostitute into the house of the LORD your God in payment for any vow, for both of these are abhorrent to the LORD your God.

5_23_19.NKJV: “You shall not charge interest to your brother—interest on money or food or anything that is lent out at interest.
5_23_19.KJV: Thou shalt not lend upon usury to thy brother; usury of money, usury of victuals, usury of any thing that is lent upon usury:
5_23_19.ESV: “You shall not charge interest on loans to your brother, interest on money, interest on food, interest on anything that is lent for interest.
5_23_19.NASB: 'You shall not charge interest to your countrymen: interest on money, food, [or] anything that may be loaned at interest.
5_23_19.NLT: “Do not charge interest on the loans you make to a fellow Israelite, whether you loan money, or food, or anything else.
5_23_19.WEB: You shall not lend on interest to your brother; interest of money, interest of food, interest of anything that is lent on interest.
5_23_19.CSB17: “Do not charge your brother interest on silver, food, or anything that can earn interest.
5_23_19.AMP: "You shall not charge interest to your fellow Israelite—interest on money, food or anything that may be loaned for interest.
5_23_19.MSG: Don't charge interest to your kinsmen on any loan: not for money or food or clothing or anything else that could earn interest.
5_23_19.NIV: Do not charge your brother interest, whether on money or food or anything else that may earn interest.
5_23_19.NRSVCE: You shall not charge interest on loans to another Israelite, interest on money, interest on provisions, interest on anything that is lent.

5_23_20.NKJV: To a foreigner you may charge interest, but to your brother you shall not charge interest, that the LORD your God may bless you in all to which you set your hand in the land which you are entering to possess.
5_23_20.KJV: Unto a stranger thou mayest lend upon usury; but unto thy brother thou shalt not lend upon usury: that the LORD thy God may bless thee in all that thou settest thine hand to in the land whither thou goest to possess it.
5_23_20.ESV: You may charge a foreigner interest, but you may not charge your brother interest, that the LORD your God may bless you in all that you undertake in the land that you are entering to take possession of it.
5_23_20.NASB: 'You may charge interest to a foreigner, but to your countrymen you shall not charge interest, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all that you undertake in the land which you are about to enter to possess.
5_23_20.NLT: You may charge interest to foreigners, but you may not charge interest to Israelites, so that the LORD your God may bless you in everything you do in the land you are about to enter and occupy.
5_23_20.WEB: You may lend on interest to a foreigner; but to your brother you shall not lend on interest, that Yahweh your God may bless you in all that you put your hand to, in the land where you go in to possess it.
5_23_20.CSB17: You may charge a foreigner interest, but you must not charge your brother Israelite interest, so that the LORD your God may bless you in everything you do in the land you are entering to possess.
5_23_20.AMP: You may charge interest to a foreigner, but to your fellow Israelite you shall not charge interest, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all that you undertake in the land which you are about to enter to possess.
5_23_20.MSG: You may charge foreigners interest, but you may not charge your brothers interest; that way God, your God, will bless all the work that you take up and the land that you are entering to possess.
5_23_20.NIV: You may charge a foreigner interest, but not a brother Israelite, so that the Lord your God may bless you in everything you put your hand to in the land you are entering to possess.
5_23_20.NRSVCE: On loans to a foreigner you may charge interest, but on loans to another Israelite you may not charge interest, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all your undertakings in the land that you are about to enter and possess.

5_23_21.NKJV: “When you make a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay to pay it; for the LORD your God will surely require it of you, and it would be sin to you.
5_23_21.KJV: When thou shalt vow a vow unto the LORD thy God, thou shalt not slack to pay it: for the LORD thy God will surely require it of thee; and it would be sin in thee.
5_23_21.ESV: “If you make a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay fulfilling it, for the LORD your God will surely require it of you, and you will be guilty of sin.
5_23_21.NASB: 'When you make a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay to pay it, for it would be sin in you, and the LORD your God will surely require it of you.
5_23_21.NLT: “When you make a vow to the LORD your God, be prompt in fulfilling whatever you promised him. For the LORD your God demands that you promptly fulfill all your vows, or you will be guilty of sin.
5_23_21.WEB: When you vow a vow to Yahweh your God, you shall not be slack to pay it; for Yahweh your God will surely require it of you; and it would be sin in you.
5_23_21.CSB17: “If you make a vow to the LORD your God, do not be slow to keep it, because he will require it of you, and it will be counted against you as sin.
5_23_21.AMP: "When you make a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay to pay it, for He will most certainly require it of you, and a delay would cause you to sin.
5_23_21.MSG: When you make a vow to God, your God, don't put off keeping it; God, your God, expects you to keep it and if you don't you're guilty.
5_23_21.NIV: If you make a vow to the Lord your God, do not be slow to pay it, for the Lord your God will certainly demand it of you and you will be guilty of sin.
5_23_21.NRSVCE: If you make a vow to the LORD your God, do not postpone fulfilling it; for the LORD your God will surely require it of you, and you would incur guilt.

5_23_22.NKJV: But if you abstain from vowing, it shall not be sin to you.
5_23_22.KJV: But if thou shalt forbear to vow, it shall be no sin in thee.
5_23_22.ESV: But if you refrain from vowing, you will not be guilty of sin.
5_23_22.NASB: 'However, if you refrain from vowing, it would not be sin in you.
5_23_22.NLT: However, it is not a sin to refrain from making a vow.
5_23_22.WEB: But if you refrain from making a vow, it shall be no sin in you.
5_23_22.CSB17: But if you refrain from making a vow, it will not be counted against you as sin.
5_23_22.AMP: But if you refrain from making a vow, that would not be [counted as] sin in you.
5_23_22.MSG: But if you don't make a vow in the first place, there's no sin.
5_23_22.NIV: But if you refrain from making a vow, you will not be guilty.
5_23_22.NRSVCE: But if you refrain from vowing, you will not incur guilt.

5_23_23.NKJV: That which has gone from your lips you shall keep and perform, for you voluntarily vowed to the LORD your God what you have promised with your mouth.
5_23_23.KJV: That which is gone out of thy lips thou shalt keep and perform; even a freewill offering, according as thou hast vowed unto the LORD thy God, which thou hast promised with thy mouth.
5_23_23.ESV: You shall be careful to do what has passed your lips, for you have voluntarily vowed to the LORD your God what you have promised with your mouth.
5_23_23.NASB: 'You shall be careful to perform what goes out from your lips, just as you have voluntarily vowed to the LORD your God, what you have promised.
5_23_23.NLT: But once you have voluntarily made a vow, be careful to fulfill your promise to the LORD your God.
5_23_23.WEB: You shall observe and do that which has gone out of your lips. Whatever you have vowed to Yahweh your God as a freewill offering, which you have promised with your mouth, you must do.
5_23_23.CSB17: Be careful to do whatever comes from your lips, because you have freely vowed what you promised to the LORD your God.
5_23_23.AMP: You shall be careful to perform that [vow] which passes your lips, just as you have made a voluntary vow to the LORD your God, just as you have promised with your own words (mouth).
5_23_23.MSG: If you say you're going to do something, do it. Keep the vow you willingly vowed to God, your God. You promised it, so do it.
5_23_23.NIV: Whatever your lips utter you must be sure to do, because you made your vow freely to the Lord your God with your own mouth.
5_23_23.NRSVCE: Whatever your lips utter you must diligently perform, just as you have freely vowed to the LORD your God with your own mouth.

5_23_24.NKJV: “When you come into your neighbor’s vineyard, you may eat your fill of grapes at your pleasure, but you shall not put any in your container.
5_23_24.KJV: When thou comest into thy neighbour's vineyard, then thou mayest eat grapes thy fill at thine own pleasure; but thou shalt not put any in thy vessel.
5_23_24.ESV: “If you go into your neighbor's vineyard, you may eat your fill of grapes, as many as you wish, but you shall not put any in your bag.
5_23_24.NASB: 'When you enter your neighbor's vineyard, then you may eat grapes until you are fully satisfied, but you shall not put any in your basket.
5_23_24.NLT: “When you enter your neighbor’s vineyard, you may eat your fill of grapes, but you must not carry any away in a basket.
5_23_24.WEB: When you come into your neighbor’s vineyard, then you may eat of grapes your fill at your own pleasure; but you shall not put any in your container.
5_23_24.CSB17: “When you enter your neighbor’s vineyard, you may eat as many grapes as you want until you are full, but do not put any in your container.
5_23_24.AMP: "When you enter your neighbor's vineyard, you may eat your fill of grapes, as many as you please, but you shall not put any in your basket [to take with you].
5_23_24.MSG: When you enter your neighbor's vineyard, you may eat all the grapes you want until you're full, but you may not put any in your bucket or bag.
5_23_24.NIV: If you enter your neighbor’s vineyard, you may eat all the grapes you want, but do not put any in your basket.
5_23_24.NRSVCE: If you go into your neighbor’s vineyard, you may eat your fill of grapes, as many as you wish, but you shall not put any in a container.

5_23_25.NKJV: When you come into your neighbor’s standing grain, you may pluck the heads with your hand, but you shall not use a sickle on your neighbor’s standing grain.
5_23_25.KJV: When thou comest into the standing corn of thy neighbour, then thou mayest pluck the ears with thine hand; but thou shalt not move a sickle unto thy neighbour's standing corn.
5_23_25.ESV: If you go into your neighbor's standing grain, you may pluck the ears with your hand, but you shall not put a sickle to your neighbor's standing grain.
5_23_25.NASB: 'When you enter your neighbor's standing grain, then you may pluck the heads with your hand, but you shall not wield a sickle in your neighbor's standing grain.
5_23_25.NLT: And when you enter your neighbor’s field of grain, you may pluck the heads of grain with your hand, but you must not harvest it with a sickle.
5_23_25.WEB: When you come into your neighbor’s standing grain, then you may pluck the ears with your hand; but you shall not move a sickle to your neighbor’s standing grain.
5_23_25.CSB17: When you enter your neighbor’s standing grain, you may pluck heads of grain with your hand, but do not put a sickle to your neighbor’s grain.
5_23_25.AMP: "When you come into the standing grain of your neighbor, you may pluck the ears of grain with your hand, but you shall not wield a sickle in your neighbor's standing grain [to harvest it].
5_23_25.MSG: And when you walk through the ripe grain of your neighbor, you may pick the heads of grain, but you may not swing your sickle there.
5_23_25.NIV: If you enter your neighbor’s grainfield, you may pick kernels with your hands, but you must not put a sickle to his standing grain.
5_23_25.NRSVCE: If you go into your neighbor’s standing grain, you may pluck the ears with your hand, but you shall not put a sickle to your neighbor’s standing grain.

5_24_1.NKJV: “When a man takes a wife and marries her, and it happens that she finds no favor in his eyes because he has found some uncleanness in her, and he writes her a certificate of divorce, puts it in her hand, and sends her out of his house,
5_24_1.KJV: When a man hath taken a wife, and married her, and it come to pass that she find no favour in his eyes, because he hath found some uncleanness in her: then let him write her a bill of divorcement, and give it in her hand, and send her out of his house. some: Heb. matter of nakedness divorcement: Heb. cutting off
5_24_1.ESV: “When a man takes a wife and marries her, if then she finds no favor in his eyes because he has found some indecency in her, and he writes her a certificate of divorce and puts it in her hand and sends her out of his house, and she departs out of his house,
5_24_1.NASB: 'When a man takes a wife and marries her, and it happens that she finds no favor in his eyes because he has found some indecency in her, and he writes her a certificate of divorce and puts [it] in her hand and sends her out from his house,
5_24_1.NLT: “Suppose a man marries a woman but she does not please him. Having discovered something wrong with her, he writes a document of divorce, hands it to her, and sends her away from his house.
5_24_1.WEB: When a man takes a wife and marries her, then it shall be, if she finds no favor in his eyes, because he has found some unseemly thing in her, that he shall write her a bill of divorce, and put it in her hand, and send her out of his house.
5_24_1.CSB17: “If a man marries a woman, but she becomes displeasing to him because he finds something indecent about her, he may write her a divorce certificate, hand it to her, and send her away from his house.
5_24_1.AMP: "When a man takes a wife and marries her, and it happens that she loses his favor because he has found something indecent or unacceptable about her, and he writes her a certificate of divorce, puts it in her hand and sends her out of his house,
5_24_1.MSG: If a man marries a woman and then it happens that he no longer likes her because he has found something wrong with her, he may give her divorce papers, put them in her hand, and send her off.
5_24_1.NIV: If a man marries a woman who becomes displeasing to him because he finds something indecent about her, and he writes her a certificate of divorce, gives it to her and sends her from his house,
5_24_1.NRSVCE: Suppose a man enters into marriage with a woman, but she does not please him because he finds something objectionable about her, and so he writes her a certificate of divorce, puts it in her hand, and sends her out of his house; she then leaves his house

5_24_2.NKJV: when she has departed from his house, and goes and becomes another man’s wife,
5_24_2.KJV: And when she is departed out of his house, she may go and be another man's wife.
5_24_2.ESV: and if she goes and becomes another man's wife,
5_24_2.NASB: and she leaves his house and goes and becomes another man's [wife],
5_24_2.NLT: When she leaves his house, she is free to marry another man.
5_24_2.WEB: When she has departed out of his house, she may go and be another man’s wife.
5_24_2.CSB17: If after leaving his house she goes and becomes another man’s wife,
5_24_2.AMP: and after she leaves his house, she goes and becomes another man's wife,
5_24_2.MSG: After she leaves, if she becomes another man's wife
5_24_2.NIV: and if after she leaves his house she becomes the wife of another man,
5_24_2.NRSVCE: and goes off to become another man’s wife.

5_24_3.NKJV: if the latter husband detests her and writes her a certificate of divorce, puts it in her hand, and sends her out of his house, or if the latter husband dies who took her as his wife,
5_24_3.KJV: And if the latter husband hate her, and write her a bill of divorcement, and giveth it in her hand, and sendeth her out of his house; or if the latter husband die, which took her to be his wife;
5_24_3.ESV: and the latter man hates her and writes her a certificate of divorce and puts it in her hand and sends her out of his house, or if the latter man dies, who took her to be his wife,
5_24_3.NASB: and if the latter husband turns against her and writes her a certificate of divorce and puts [it] in her hand and sends her out of his house, or if the latter husband dies who took her to be his wife,
5_24_3.NLT: But if the second husband also turns against her, writes a document of divorce, hands it to her, and sends her away, or if he dies,
5_24_3.WEB: If the latter husband hates her, and write her a bill of divorce, and puts it in her hand, and sends her out of his house; or if the latter husband die, who took her to be his wife;
5_24_3.CSB17: and the second man hates her, writes her a divorce certificate, hands it to her, and sends her away from his house or if he dies,
5_24_3.AMP: and if the latter husband turns against her and writes her a certificate of divorce, puts it in her hand, and sends her out of his house, or if the latter husband dies who took her as his wife,
5_24_3.MSG: and he also comes to hate her and this second husband also gives her divorce papers, puts them in her hand, and sends her off, or if he should die,
5_24_3.NIV: and her second husband dislikes her and writes her a certificate of divorce, gives it to her and sends her from his house, or if he dies,
5_24_3.NRSVCE: Then suppose the second man dislikes her, writes her a bill of divorce, puts it in her hand, and sends her out of his house (or the second man who married her dies);

5_24_4.NKJV: then her former husband who divorced her must not take her back to be his wife after she has been defiled; for that is an abomination before the LORD, and you shall not bring sin on the land which the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance.
5_24_4.KJV: Her former husband, which sent her away, may not take her again to be his wife, after that she is defiled; for that is abomination before the LORD: and thou shalt not cause the land to sin, which the LORD thy God giveth thee for an inheritance.
5_24_4.ESV: then her former husband, who sent her away, may not take her again to be his wife, after she has been defiled, for that is an abomination before the LORD. And you shall not bring sin upon the land that the LORD your God is giving you for an inheritance.
5_24_4.NASB: [then] her former husband who sent her away is not allowed to take her again to be his wife, since she has been defiled; for that is an abomination before the LORD, and you shall not bring sin on the land which the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance.
5_24_4.NLT: the first husband may not marry her again, for she has been defiled. That would be detestable to the LORD. You must not bring guilt upon the land the LORD your God is giving you as a special possession.
5_24_4.WEB: her former husband, who sent her away, may not take her again to be his wife, after that she is defiled; for that is abomination before Yahweh. You shall not cause the land to sin, which Yahweh your God gives you for an inheritance.
5_24_4.CSB17: the first husband who sent her away may not marry her again after she has been defiled, because that would be detestable to the LORD. You must not bring guilt on the land the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance.
5_24_4.AMP: then her former husband who [first] sent her away may not take her again as his wife, since she has been defiled; for that is an outrage before the LORD, and you shall not bring sin on the land which the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance.
5_24_4.MSG: then the first husband who divorced her can't marry her again. She has made herself ritually unclean, and her remarriage would be an abomination in the Presence of God and defile the land with sin, this land that God, your God, is giving you as an inheritance.
5_24_4.NIV: then her first husband, who divorced her, is not allowed to marry her again after she has been defiled. That would be detestable in the eyes of the Lord. Do not bring sin upon the land the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance.
5_24_4.NRSVCE: her first husband, who sent her away, is not permitted to take her again to be his wife after she has been defiled; for that would be abhorrent to the LORD, and you shall not bring guilt on the land that the LORD your God is giving you as a possession.

5_24_5.NKJV: “When a man has taken a new wife, he shall not go out to war or be charged with any business; he shall be free at home one year, and bring happiness to his wife whom he has taken.
5_24_5.KJV: When a man hath taken a new wife, he shall not go out to war, neither shall he be charged with any business: but he shall be free at home one year, and shall cheer up his wife which he hath taken. neither: Heb. not any thing shall pass upon him
5_24_5.ESV: “When a man is newly married, he shall not go out with the army or be liable for any other public duty. He shall be free at home one year to be happy with his wife whom he has taken.
5_24_5.NASB: 'When a man takes a new wife, he shall not go out with the army nor be charged with any duty; he shall be free at home one year and shall give happiness to his wife whom he has taken.
5_24_5.NLT: “A newly married man must not be drafted into the army or be given any other official responsibilities. He must be free to spend one year at home, bringing happiness to the wife he has married.
5_24_5.WEB: When a man takes a new wife, he shall not go out in the army, neither shall he be assigned any business. He shall be free at home one year, and shall cheer his wife whom he has taken.
5_24_5.CSB17: “When a man takes a bride, he must not go out with the army or be liable for any duty. He is free to stay at home for one year, so that he can bring joy to the wife he has married.
5_24_5.AMP: "When a man takes a new wife, he shall not go out [to fight] with the army nor be charged with any duty; he shall be free at home for one year and shall bring happiness to his wife whom he has taken.
5_24_5.MSG: When a man takes a new wife, he is not to go out with the army or be given any business or work duties. He gets one year off simply to be at home making his wife happy.
5_24_5.NIV: If a man has recently married, he must not be sent to war or have any other duty laid on him. For one year he is to be free to stay at home and bring happiness to the wife he has married.
5_24_5.NRSVCE: When a man is newly married, he shall not go out with the army or be charged with any related duty. He shall be free at home one year, to be happy with the wife whom he has married.

5_24_6.NKJV: “No man shall take the lower or the upper millstone in pledge, for he takes one’s living in pledge.
5_24_6.KJV: No man shall take the nether or the upper millstone to pledge: for he taketh a man's life to pledge.
5_24_6.ESV: “No one shall take a mill or an upper millstone in pledge, for that would be taking a life in pledge.
5_24_6.NASB: 'No one shall take a handmill or an upper millstone in pledge, for he would be taking a life in pledge.
5_24_6.NLT: “It is wrong to take a set of millstones, or even just the upper millstone, as security for a loan, for the owner uses it to make a living.
5_24_6.WEB: No man shall take the mill or the upper millstone as a pledge; for he takes a life in pledge.
5_24_6.CSB17: “Do not take a pair of grindstones or even the upper millstone as security for a debt, because that is like taking a life as security.
5_24_6.AMP: "No one shall take a handmill or an upper millstone [used to grind grain into bread] as security [for a debt], for he would be taking a [person's] life in pledge.
5_24_6.MSG: Don't seize a handmill or an upper millstone as collateral for a loan. You'd be seizing someone's very life.
5_24_6.NIV: Do not take a pair of millstones—not even the upper one—as security for a debt, because that would be taking a man’s livelihood as security.
5_24_6.NRSVCE: No one shall take a mill or an upper millstone in pledge, for that would be taking a life in pledge.

5_24_7.NKJV: “If a man is found kidnapping any of his brethren of the children of Israel, and mistreats him or sells him, then that kidnapper shall die; and you shall put away the evil from among you.
5_24_7.KJV: If a man be found stealing any of his brethren of the children of Israel, and maketh merchandise of him, or selleth him; then that thief shall die; and thou shalt put evil away from among you.
5_24_7.ESV: “If a man is found stealing one of his brothers of the people of Israel, and if he treats him as a slave or sells him, then that thief shall die. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.
5_24_7.NASB: 'If a man is caught kidnapping any of his countrymen of the sons of Israel, and he deals with him violently or sells him, then that thief shall die; so you shall purge the evil from among you.
5_24_7.NLT: “If anyone kidnaps a fellow Israelite and treats him as a slave or sells him, the kidnapper must die. In this way, you will purge the evil from among you.
5_24_7.WEB: If a man is found stealing any of his brothers of the children of Israel, and he deals with him as a slave, or sells him; then that thief shall die. So you shall remove the evil from among you.
5_24_7.CSB17: “If a man is discovered kidnapping one of his Israelite brothers, whether he treats him as a slave or sells him, the kidnapper must die. You must purge the evil from you.
5_24_7.AMP: "If a man is caught kidnapping any of his countrymen from the sons of Israel, and he treats him violently or sells him [as a slave], then that thief shall die. So you shall remove the evil from among you.
5_24_7.MSG: If a man is caught kidnapping one of his kinsmen, someone of the People of Israel, to enslave or sell him, the kidnapper must die. Purge that evil from among you.
5_24_7.NIV: If a man is caught kidnapping one of his brother Israelites and treats him as a slave or sells him, the kidnapper must die. You must purge the evil from among you.
5_24_7.NRSVCE: If someone is caught kidnaping another Israelite, enslaving or selling the Israelite, then that kidnaper shall die. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

5_24_8.NKJV: “Take heed in an outbreak of leprosy, that you carefully observe and do according to all that the priests, the Levites, shall teach you; just as I commanded them, so you shall be careful to do.
5_24_8.KJV: Take heed in the plague of leprosy, that thou observe diligently, and do according to all that the priests the Levites shall teach you: as I commanded them, so ye shall observe to do.
5_24_8.ESV: “Take care, in a case of leprous disease, to be very careful to do according to all that the Levitical priests shall direct you. As I commanded them, so you shall be careful to do.
5_24_8.NASB: 'Be careful against an infection of leprosy, that you diligently observe and do according to all that the Levitical priests teach you; as I have commanded them, so you shall be careful to do.
5_24_8.NLT: “In all cases involving serious skin diseases, be careful to follow the instructions of the Levitical priests; obey all the commands I have given them.
5_24_8.WEB: Be careful in the plague of leprosy, that you observe diligently, and do according to all that the priests the Levites teach you. As I commanded them, so you shall observe to do.
5_24_8.CSB17: “Be careful with a person who has a case of serious skin disease, following carefully everything the Levitical priests instruct you to do. Be careful to do as I have commanded them.
5_24_8.AMP: "Be careful during an outbreak of leprosy, that you diligently observe and do according to all that the Levitical priests teach you; just as I have commanded them, so you shall be careful to do.
5_24_8.MSG: Warning! If a serious skin disease breaks out, follow exactly the rules set down by the Levitical priests. Follow them precisely as I commanded them.
5_24_8.NIV: In cases of leprous diseases be very careful to do exactly as the priests, who are Levites, instruct you. You must follow carefully what I have commanded them.
5_24_8.NRSVCE: Guard against an outbreak of a leprous skin disease by being very careful; you shall carefully observe whatever the levitical priests instruct you, just as I have commanded them.

5_24_9.NKJV: Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam on the way when you came out of Egypt!
5_24_9.KJV: Remember what the LORD thy God did unto Miriam by the way, after that ye were come forth out of Egypt.
5_24_9.ESV: Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam on the way as you came out of Egypt.
5_24_9.NASB: 'Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam on the way as you came out of Egypt.
5_24_9.NLT: Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam as you were coming from Egypt.
5_24_9.WEB: Remember what Yahweh your God did to Miriam, by the way as you came out of Egypt.
5_24_9.CSB17: Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam on the journey after you left Egypt.
5_24_9.AMP: Remember [with thoughtful concern] what the LORD your God did to Miriam on the road as you came out of Egypt.
5_24_9.MSG: Don't forget what God, your God, did to Miriam on your way out of Egypt.
5_24_9.NIV: Remember what the Lord your God did to Miriam along the way after you came out of Egypt.
5_24_9.NRSVCE: Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam on your journey out of Egypt.

5_24_10.NKJV: “When you lend your brother anything, you shall not go into his house to get his pledge.
5_24_10.KJV: When thou dost lend thy brother any thing, thou shalt not go into his house to fetch his pledge. lend: Heb. lend the loan of any thing to, etc
5_24_10.ESV: “When you make your neighbor a loan of any sort, you shall not go into his house to collect his pledge.
5_24_10.NASB: 'When you make your neighbor a loan of any sort, you shall not enter his house to take his pledge.
5_24_10.NLT: “If you lend anything to your neighbor, do not enter his house to pick up the item he is giving as security.
5_24_10.WEB: When you lend your neighbor any kind of loan, you shall not go into his house to get his pledge.
5_24_10.CSB17: “When you make a loan of any kind to your neighbor, do not enter his house to collect what he offers as security.
5_24_10.AMP: "When you lend your neighbor anything, you shall not go into his house to get his pledge (security deposit).
5_24_10.MSG: When you make a loan of any kind to your neighbor, don't enter his house to claim his pledge.
5_24_10.NIV: When you make a loan of any kind to your neighbor, do not go into his house to get what he is offering as a pledge.
5_24_10.NRSVCE: When you make your neighbor a loan of any kind, you shall not go into the house to take the pledge.

5_24_11.NKJV: You shall stand outside, and the man to whom you lend shall bring the pledge out to you.
5_24_11.KJV: Thou shalt stand abroad, and the man to whom thou dost lend shall bring out the pledge abroad unto thee.
5_24_11.ESV: You shall stand outside, and the man to whom you make the loan shall bring the pledge out to you.
5_24_11.NASB: 'You shall remain outside, and the man to whom you make the loan shall bring the pledge out to you.
5_24_11.NLT: You must wait outside while he goes in and brings it out to you.
5_24_11.WEB: You shall stand outside, and the man to whom you lend shall bring the pledge outside to you.
5_24_11.CSB17: Stand outside while the man you are making the loan to brings the security out to you.
5_24_11.AMP: You shall stand outside, and the man to whom you lend shall bring the pledge out to you.
5_24_11.MSG: Wait outside. Let the man to whom you made the pledge bring the pledge to you outside.
5_24_11.NIV: Stay outside and let the man to whom you are making the loan bring the pledge out to you.
5_24_11.NRSVCE: You shall wait outside, while the person to whom you are making the loan brings the pledge out to you.

5_24_12.NKJV: And if the man is poor, you shall not keep his pledge overnight.
5_24_12.KJV: And if the man be poor, thou shalt not sleep with his pledge:
5_24_12.ESV: And if he is a poor man, you shall not sleep in his pledge.
5_24_12.NASB: 'If he is a poor man, you shall not sleep with his pledge.
5_24_12.NLT: If your neighbor is poor and gives you his cloak as security for a loan, do not keep the cloak overnight.
5_24_12.WEB: If he is a poor man, you shall not sleep with his pledge.
5_24_12.CSB17: If he is a poor man, do not sleep with the garment he has given as security.
5_24_12.AMP: If the man is poor, you shall not keep his pledge overnight.
5_24_12.MSG: And if he is destitute, don't use his cloak as a bedroll;
5_24_12.NIV: If the man is poor, do not go to sleep with his pledge in your possession.
5_24_12.NRSVCE: If the person is poor, you shall not sleep in the garment given you as the pledge.

5_24_13.NKJV: You shall in any case return the pledge to him again when the sun goes down, that he may sleep in his own garment and bless you; and it shall be righteousness to you before the LORD your God.
5_24_13.KJV: In any case thou shalt deliver him the pledge again when the sun goeth down, that he may sleep in his own raiment, and bless thee: and it shall be righteousness unto thee before the LORD thy God.
5_24_13.ESV: You shall restore to him the pledge as the sun sets, that he may sleep in his cloak and bless you. And it shall be righteousness for you before the LORD your God.
5_24_13.NASB: 'When the sun goes down you shall surely return the pledge to him, that he may sleep in his cloak and bless you; and it will be righteousness for you before the LORD your God.
5_24_13.NLT: Return the cloak to its owner by sunset so he can stay warm through the night and bless you, and the LORD your God will count you as righteous.
5_24_13.WEB: You shall surely restore to him the pledge when the sun goes down, that he may sleep in his garment, and bless you. It shall be righteousness to you before Yahweh your God.
5_24_13.CSB17: Be sure to return it to him at sunset. Then he will sleep in it and bless you, and this will be counted as righteousness to you before the LORD your God.
5_24_13.AMP: You shall certainly restore the pledge (security deposit) to him at sunset, so that he may sleep in his garment and bless you; and it will be credited to you as righteousness (right standing) before the LORD your God.
5_24_13.MSG: return it to him at nightfall so that he can sleep in his cloak and bless you. In the sight of God, your God, that will be viewed as a righteous act.
5_24_13.NIV: Return his cloak to him by sunset so that he may sleep in it. Then he will thank you, and it will be regarded as a righteous act in the sight of the Lord your God.
5_24_13.NRSVCE: You shall give the pledge back by sunset, so that your neighbor may sleep in the cloak and bless you; and it will be to your credit before the LORD your God.

5_24_14.NKJV: “You shall not oppress a hired servant who is poor and needy, whether one of your brethren or one of the aliens who is in your land within your gates.
5_24_14.KJV: Thou shalt not oppress an hired servant that is poor and needy, whether he be of thy brethren, or of thy strangers that are in thy land within thy gates:
5_24_14.ESV: “You shall not oppress a hired worker who is poor and needy, whether he is one of your brothers or one of the sojourners who are in your land within your towns.
5_24_14.NASB: 'You shall not oppress a hired servant [who is] poor and needy, whether [he is] one of your countrymen or one of your aliens who is in your land in your towns.
5_24_14.NLT: “Never take advantage of poor and destitute laborers, whether they are fellow Israelites or foreigners living in your towns.
5_24_14.WEB: You shall not oppress a hired servant who is poor and needy, whether he is one of your brothers, or one of the foreigners who are in your land within your gates.
5_24_14.CSB17: “Do not oppress a hired worker who is poor and needy, whether one of your Israelite brothers or one of the resident aliens in a town in your land.
5_24_14.AMP: "You shall not take advantage of a hired servant who is poor and needy, whether [he is] one of your countrymen or one of the strangers (resident aliens, foreigners) who is in your land inside your cities.
5_24_14.MSG: Don't abuse a laborer who is destitute and needy, whether he is a fellow Israelite living in your land and in your city.
5_24_14.NIV: Do not take advantage of a hired man who is poor and needy, whether he is a brother Israelite or an alien living in one of your towns.
5_24_14.NRSVCE: You shall not withhold the wages of poor and needy laborers, whether other Israelites or aliens who reside in your land in one of your towns.

5_24_15.NKJV: Each day you shall give him his wages, and not let the sun go down on it, for he is poor and has set his heart on it; lest he cry out against you to the LORD, and it be sin to you.
5_24_15.KJV: At his day thou shalt give him his hire, neither shall the sun go down upon it; for he is poor, and setteth his heart upon it: lest he cry against thee unto the LORD, and it be sin unto thee. setteth: Heb. lifteth his soul unto it
5_24_15.ESV: You shall give him his wages on the same day, before the sun sets (for he is poor and counts on it), lest he cry against you to the LORD, and you be guilty of sin.
5_24_15.NASB: 'You shall give him his wages on his day before the sun sets, for he is poor and sets his heart on it; so that he will not cry against you to the LORD and it become sin in you.
5_24_15.NLT: You must pay them their wages each day before sunset because they are poor and are counting on it. If you don’t, they might cry out to the LORD against you, and it would be counted against you as sin.
5_24_15.WEB: In his day you shall give him his hire, neither shall the sun go down on it; for he is poor, and sets his heart on it; lest he cry against you to Yahweh, and it be sin to you.
5_24_15.CSB17: You are to pay him his wages each day before the sun sets, because he is poor and depends on them. Otherwise he will cry out to the LORD against you, and you will be held guilty.
5_24_15.AMP: You shall give him his wages on the day that he earns them before the sun sets—for he is poor and is counting on it—so that he does not cry out to the LORD against you, and it becomes a sin for you.
5_24_15.MSG: Pay him at the end of each workday; he's living from hand to mouth and needs it now. If you hold back his pay, he'll protest to God and you'll have sin on your books.
5_24_15.NIV: Pay him his wages each day before sunset, because he is poor and is counting on it. Otherwise he may cry to the Lord against you, and you will be guilty of sin.
5_24_15.NRSVCE: You shall pay them their wages daily before sunset, because they are poor and their livelihood depends on them; otherwise they might cry to the LORD against you, and you would incur guilt.

5_24_16.NKJV: “Fathers shall not be put to death for their children, nor shall children be put to death for their fathers; a person shall be put to death for his own sin.
5_24_16.KJV: The fathers shall not be put to death for the children, neither shall the children be put to death for the fathers: every man shall be put to death for his own sin.
5_24_16.ESV: “Fathers shall not be put to death because of their children, nor shall children be put to death because of their fathers. Each one shall be put to death for his own sin.
5_24_16.NASB: 'Fathers shall not be put to death for [their] sons, nor shall sons be put to death for [their] fathers; everyone shall be put to death for his own sin.
5_24_16.NLT: “Parents must not be put to death for the sins of their children, nor children for the sins of their parents. Those deserving to die must be put to death for their own crimes.
5_24_16.WEB: The fathers shall not be put to death for the children, neither shall the children be put to death for the fathers. Every man shall be put to death for his own sin.
5_24_16.CSB17: “Fathers are not to be put to death for their children, and children are not to be put to death for their fathers; each person will be put to death for his own sin.
5_24_16.AMP: "The fathers shall not be put to death for [the sins of] their children, nor shall the children be put to death for their fathers; [only] for his own sin shall anyone be put to death.
5_24_16.MSG: Parents shall not be put to death for their children, nor children for their parents. Each person shall be put to death for his own sin.
5_24_16.NIV: Fathers shall not be put to death for their children, nor children put to death for their fathers; each is to die for his own sin.
5_24_16.NRSVCE: Parents shall not be put to death for their children, nor shall children be put to death for their parents; only for their own crimes may persons be put to death.

5_24_17.NKJV: “You shall not pervert justice due the stranger or the fatherless, nor take a widow’s garment as a pledge.
5_24_17.KJV: Thou shalt not pervert the judgment of the stranger, nor of the fatherless; nor take a widow's raiment to pledge:
5_24_17.ESV: “You shall not pervert the justice due to the sojourner or to the fatherless, or take a widow's garment in pledge,
5_24_17.NASB: 'You shall not pervert the justice due an alien [or] an orphan, nor take a widow's garment in pledge.
5_24_17.NLT: “True justice must be given to foreigners living among you and to orphans, and you must never accept a widow’s garment as security for her debt.
5_24_17.WEB: You shall not deprive the foreigner or the fatherless of justice, nor take a widow’s clothing in pledge;
5_24_17.CSB17: Do not deny justice to a resident alien or fatherless child, and do not take a widow’s garment as security.
5_24_17.AMP: "You shall not pervert the justice due a stranger or an orphan, nor seize (impound) a widow's garment as security [for a loan].
5_24_17.MSG: Make sure foreigners and orphans get their just rights. Don't take the cloak of a widow as security for a loan.
5_24_17.NIV: Do not deprive the alien or the fatherless of justice, or take the cloak of the widow as a pledge.
5_24_17.NRSVCE: You shall not deprive a resident alien or an orphan of justice; you shall not take a widow’s garment in pledge.

5_24_18.NKJV: But you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt, and the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I command you to do this thing.
5_24_18.KJV: But thou shalt remember that thou wast a bondman in Egypt, and the LORD thy God redeemed thee thence: therefore I command thee to do this thing.
5_24_18.ESV: but you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt and the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I command you to do this.
5_24_18.NASB: 'But you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt, and that the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I am commanding you to do this thing.
5_24_18.NLT: Always remember that you were slaves in Egypt and that the LORD your God redeemed you from your slavery. That is why I have given you this command.
5_24_18.WEB: but you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt, and Yahweh your God redeemed you there. Therefore I command you to do this thing.
5_24_18.CSB17: Remember that you were a slave in Egypt, and the LORD your God redeemed you from there. Therefore I am commanding you to do this.
5_24_18.AMP: But you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt, and the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I am commanding you to do this thing.
5_24_18.MSG: Don't ever forget that you were once slaves in Egypt and God, your God, got you out of there. I command you: Do what I'm telling you.
5_24_18.NIV: Remember that you were slaves in Egypt and the Lord your God redeemed you from there. That is why I command you to do this.
5_24_18.NRSVCE: Remember that you were a slave in Egypt and the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I command you to do this.

5_24_19.NKJV: “When you reap your harvest in your field, and forget a sheaf in the field, you shall not go back to get it; it shall be for the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow, that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
5_24_19.KJV: When thou cuttest down thine harvest in thy field, and hast forgot a sheaf in the field, thou shalt not go again to fetch it: it shall be for the stranger, for the fatherless, and for the widow: that the LORD thy God may bless thee in all the work of thine hands.
5_24_19.ESV: “When you reap your harvest in your field and forget a sheaf in the field, you shall not go back to get it. It shall be for the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow, that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
5_24_19.NASB: 'When you reap your harvest in your field and have forgotten a sheaf in the field, you shall not go back to get it; it shall be for the alien, for the orphan, and for the widow, in order that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
5_24_19.NLT: “When you are harvesting your crops and forget to bring in a bundle of grain from your field, don’t go back to get it. Leave it for the foreigners, orphans, and widows. Then the LORD your God will bless you in all you do.
5_24_19.WEB: When you reap your harvest in your field, and have forgotten a sheaf in the field, you shall not go again to get it. It shall be for the foreigner, for the fatherless, and for the widow; that Yahweh your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
5_24_19.CSB17: “When you reap the harvest in your field, and you forget a sheaf in the field, do not go back to get it. It is to be left for the resident alien, the fatherless, and the widow, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
5_24_19.AMP: " When you reap your harvest in your field and have forgotten a sheaf [of grain] in the field, you shall not go back to get it; it shall be for the stranger, for the orphan, and for the widow, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
5_24_19.MSG: When you harvest your grain and forget a sheaf back in the field, don't go back and get it; leave it for the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow so that God, your God, will bless you in all your work.
5_24_19.NIV: When you are harvesting in your field and you overlook a sheaf, do not go back to get it. Leave it for the alien, the fatherless and the widow, so that the Lord your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
5_24_19.NRSVCE: When you reap your harvest in your field and forget a sheaf in the field, you shall not go back to get it; it shall be left for the alien, the orphan, and the widow, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all your undertakings.

5_24_20.NKJV: When you beat your olive trees, you shall not go over the boughs again; it shall be for the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow.
5_24_20.KJV: When thou beatest thine olive tree, thou shalt not go over the boughs again: it shall be for the stranger, for the fatherless, and for the widow. go: Heb. bough it after thee
5_24_20.ESV: When you beat your olive trees, you shall not go over them again. It shall be for the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow.
5_24_20.NASB: 'When you beat your olive tree, you shall not go over the boughs again; it shall be for the alien, for the orphan, and for the widow.
5_24_20.NLT: When you beat the olives from your olive trees, don’t go over the boughs twice. Leave the remaining olives for the foreigners, orphans, and widows.
5_24_20.WEB: When you beat your olive tree, you shall not go over the boughs again. It shall be for the foreigner, for the fatherless, and for the widow.
5_24_20.CSB17: When you knock down the fruit from your olive tree, do not go over the branches again. What remains will be for the resident alien, the fatherless, and the widow.
5_24_20.AMP: When you beat [the olives off of] your olive tree, do not search through the branches again; [whatever is left] shall be for the stranger, for the orphan, and for the widow.
5_24_20.MSG: When you shake the olives off your trees, don't go back over the branches and strip them bare—what's left is for the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow.
5_24_20.NIV: When you beat the olives from your trees, do not go over the branches a second time. Leave what remains for the alien, the fatherless and the widow.
5_24_20.NRSVCE: When you beat your olive trees, do not strip what is left; it shall be for the alien, the orphan, and the widow.

5_24_21.NKJV: When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, you shall not glean it afterward; it shall be for the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow.
5_24_21.KJV: When thou gatherest the grapes of thy vineyard, thou shalt not glean it afterward: it shall be for the stranger, for the fatherless, and for the widow. afterward: Heb. after thee
5_24_21.ESV: When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, you shall not strip it afterward. It shall be for the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow.
5_24_21.NASB: 'When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, you shall not go over it again; it shall be for the alien, for the orphan, and for the widow.
5_24_21.NLT: When you gather the grapes in your vineyard, don’t glean the vines after they are picked. Leave the remaining grapes for the foreigners, orphans, and widows.
5_24_21.WEB: When you harvest your vineyard, you shall not glean it after yourselves. It shall be for the foreigner, for the fatherless, and for the widow.
5_24_21.CSB17: When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, do not glean what is left. What remains will be for the resident alien, the fatherless, and the widow.
5_24_21.AMP: "When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, you shall not glean it afterward; it shall be for the stranger, for the orphan, and for the widow.
5_24_21.MSG: And when you cut the grapes in your vineyard, don't take every last grape—leave a few for the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow.
5_24_21.NIV: When you harvest the grapes in your vineyard, do not go over the vines again. Leave what remains for the alien, the fatherless and the widow.
5_24_21.NRSVCE: When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, do not glean what is left; it shall be for the alien, the orphan, and the widow.

5_24_22.NKJV: And you shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt; therefore I command you to do this thing.
5_24_22.KJV: And thou shalt remember that thou wast a bondman in the land of Egypt: therefore I command thee to do this thing.
5_24_22.ESV: You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt; therefore I command you to do this.
5_24_22.NASB: 'You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt; therefore I am commanding you to do this thing.
5_24_22.NLT: Remember that you were slaves in the land of Egypt. That is why I am giving you this command.
5_24_22.WEB: You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt. Therefore I command you to do this thing.
5_24_22.CSB17: Remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt. Therefore I am commanding you to do this.
5_24_22.AMP: You shall [thoughtfully] remember [the fact] that you were a slave in the land of Egypt; therefore I am commanding you to do this thing.
5_24_22.MSG: Don't ever forget that you were a slave in Egypt. I command you: Do what I'm telling you.
5_24_22.NIV: Remember that you were slaves in Egypt. That is why I command you to do this.
5_24_22.NRSVCE: Remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt; therefore I am commanding you to do this.

5_25_1.NKJV: “If there is a dispute between men, and they come to court, that the judges may judge them, and they justify the righteous and condemn the wicked,
5_25_1.KJV: If there be a controversy between men, and they come unto judgment, that the judges may judge them; then they shall justify the righteous, and condemn the wicked.
5_25_1.ESV: “If there is a dispute between men and they come into court and the judges decide between them, acquitting the innocent and condemning the guilty,
5_25_1.NASB: 'If there is a dispute between men and they go to court, and the judges decide their case, and they justify the righteous and condemn the wicked,
5_25_1.NLT: “Suppose two people take a dispute to court, and the judges declare that one is right and the other is wrong.
5_25_1.WEB: If there is a controversy between men, and they come to judgment, and the judges judge them; then they shall justify the righteous, and condemn the wicked.
5_25_1.CSB17: “If there is a dispute between men, they are to go to court, and the judges will hear their case. They will clear the innocent and condemn the guilty.
5_25_1.AMP: "If there is a controversy between men, and they go to court, and the judges decide [the issue] between them, and they judge in favor of the innocent and condemn the guilty,
5_25_1.MSG: When men have a legal dispute, let them go to court; the judges will decide between them, declaring one innocent and the other guilty.
5_25_1.NIV: When men have a dispute, they are to take it to court and the judges will decide the case, acquitting the innocent and condemning the guilty.
5_25_1.NRSVCE: Suppose two persons have a dispute and enter into litigation, and the judges decide between them, declaring one to be in the right and the other to be in the wrong.

5_25_2.NKJV: then it shall be, if the wicked man deserves to be beaten, that the judge will cause him to lie down and be beaten in his presence, according to his guilt, with a certain number of blows.
5_25_2.KJV: And it shall be, if the wicked man be worthy to be beaten, that the judge shall cause him to lie down, and to be beaten before his face, according to his fault, by a certain number.
5_25_2.ESV: then if the guilty man deserves to be beaten, the judge shall cause him to lie down and be beaten in his presence with a number of stripes in proportion to his offense.
5_25_2.NASB: then it shall be if the wicked man deserves to be beaten, the judge shall then make him lie down and be beaten in his presence with the number of stripes according to his guilt.
5_25_2.NLT: If the person in the wrong is sentenced to be flogged, the judge must command him to lie down and be beaten in his presence with the number of lashes appropriate to the crime.
5_25_2.WEB: It shall be, if the wicked man is worthy to be beaten, that the judge shall cause him to lie down, and to be beaten before his face, according to his wickedness, by number.
5_25_2.CSB17: If the guilty party deserves to be flogged, the judge will make him lie down and be flogged in his presence with the number of lashes appropriate for his crime.
5_25_2.AMP: then it shall be that if the guilty man deserves to be beaten, the judge shall make him lie down and be beaten in his presence with a [certain] number of stripes in proportion to his offense.
5_25_2.MSG: If the guilty one deserves punishment, the judge will have him prostrate himself before him and lashed as many times as his crime deserves,
5_25_2.NIV: If the guilty man deserves to be beaten, the judge shall make him lie down and have him flogged in his presence with the number of lashes his crime deserves,
5_25_2.NRSVCE: If the one in the wrong deserves to be flogged, the judge shall make that person lie down and be beaten in his presence with the number of lashes proportionate to the offense.

5_25_3.NKJV: Forty blows he may give him and no more, lest he should exceed this and beat him with many blows above these, and your brother be humiliated in your sight.
5_25_3.KJV: Forty stripes he may give him, and not exceed: lest, if he should exceed, and beat him above these with many stripes, then thy brother should seem vile unto thee.
5_25_3.ESV: Forty stripes may be given him, but not more, lest, if one should go on to beat him with more stripes than these, your brother be degraded in your sight.
5_25_3.NASB: 'He may beat him forty times [but] no more, so that he does not beat him with many more stripes than these and your brother is not degraded in your eyes.
5_25_3.NLT: But never give more than forty lashes; more than forty lashes would publicly humiliate your neighbor.
5_25_3.WEB: He may sentence him to no more than forty stripes. He shall not give more; lest, if he should give more, and beat him more than that many stripes, then your brother will be degraded in your sight.
5_25_3.CSB17: He may be flogged with forty lashes, but no more. Otherwise, if he is flogged with more lashes than these, your brother will be degraded in your sight.
5_25_3.AMP: He may have him beaten forty times, but no more. He is not to be beaten with more stripes than these and he is not to be degraded [that is, treated like an animal] in your sight.
5_25_3.MSG: but not more than forty. If you hit him more than forty times, you will degrade him to something less than human.
5_25_3.NIV: but he must not give him more than forty lashes. If he is flogged more than that, your brother will be degraded in your eyes.
5_25_3.NRSVCE: Forty lashes may be given but not more; if more lashes than these are given, your neighbor will be degraded in your sight.

5_25_4.NKJV: “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain.
5_25_4.KJV: Thou shalt not muzzle the ox when he treadeth out the corn. treadeth: Heb. thresheth

5_25_5.NKJV: “If brothers dwell together, and one of them dies and has no son, the widow of the dead man shall not be married to a stranger outside the family; her husband’s brother shall go in to her, take her as his wife, and perform the duty of a husband’s brother to her.
5_25_5.KJV: If brethren dwell together, and one of them die, and have no child, the wife of the dead shall not marry without unto a stranger : her husband's brother shall go in unto her, and take her to him to wife, and perform the duty of an husband's brother unto her. her husband's: or, her next kinsman
5_25_5.ESV: “If brothers dwell together, and one of them dies and has no son, the wife of the dead man shall not be married outside the family to a stranger. Her husband's brother shall go in to her and take her as his wife and perform the duty of a husband's brother to her.

5_25_5.NLT: “If two brothers are living together on the same property and one of them dies without a son, his widow may not be married to anyone from outside the family. Instead, her husband’s brother should marry her and have intercourse with her to fulfill the duties of a brother-in-law.

5_25_5.AMP: "If brothers are living together and one of them dies without a son, the widow of the deceased shall not be married outside the family to a stranger. Her husband's brother shall be intimate with her after taking her as his wife and perform the duty of a husband's brother to her.

5_26_5.NKJV: And you shall answer and say before the LORD your God: ‘My father was a Syrian, about to perish, and he went down to Egypt and dwelt there, few in number; and there he became a nation, great, mighty, and populous.

5_26_5.NASB: 'You shall answer and say before the LORD your God, 'My father was a wandering Aramean, and he went down to Egypt and sojourned there, few in number; but there he became a great, mighty and populous nation.
5_26_5.NLT: “You must then say in the presence of the LORD your God, ‘My ancestor Jacob was a wandering Aramean who went to live as a foreigner in Egypt. His family arrived few in number, but in Egypt they became a large and mighty nation.

5_26_15.AMP: Look down from Your holy dwelling above, from heaven, and bless Your people Israel, and the land which You have given us, as You have sworn to our fathers, a land [of plenty] flowing with milk and honey.'
5_26_15.MSG: Look down from your holy house in Heaven!Bless your people Israel and the ground you gave us,just as you promised our ancestors you would,this land flowing with milk and honey.
5_26_15.NIV: Look down from heaven, your holy dwelling place, and bless your people Israel and the land you have given us as you promised on oath to our forefathers, a land flowing with milk and honey.”
5_26_15.NRSVCE: Look down from your holy habitation, from heaven, and bless your people Israel and the ground that you have given us, as you swore to our ancestors — a land flowing with milk and honey.”

5_26_16.NKJV: “This day the LORD your God commands you to observe these statutes and judgments; therefore you shall be careful to observe them with all your heart and with all your soul.
5_26_16.KJV: This day the LORD thy God hath commanded thee to do these statutes and judgments: thou shalt therefore keep and do them with all thine heart, and with all thy soul.
5_26_16.ESV: “This day the LORD your God commands you to do these statutes and rules. You shall therefore be careful to do them with all your heart and with all your soul.
5_26_16.NASB: 'This day the LORD your God commands you to do these statutes and ordinances. You shall therefore be careful to do them with all your heart and with all your soul.
5_26_16.NLT: “Today the LORD your God has commanded you to obey all these decrees and regulations. So be careful to obey them wholeheartedly.
5_26_16.WEB: Today Yahweh your God commands you to do these statutes and ordinances. You shall therefore keep and do them with all your heart and with all your soul.
5_26_16.CSB17: “The LORD your God is commanding you this day to follow these statutes and ordinances. Follow them carefully with all your heart and all your soul.
5_26_16.AMP: "This day the LORD your God commands you to do these statutes and judgments (precepts). Therefore, you shall be careful to do them with all your heart and with all your soul (your entire being).
5_26_16.MSG: This very day God, your God, commands you to follow these rules and regulations, to live them out with everything you have in you.
5_26_16.NIV: Follow the Lord’s CommandsThe Lord your God commands you this day to follow these decrees and laws; carefully observe them with all your heart and with all your soul.
5_26_16.NRSVCE: This very day the LORD your God is commanding you to observe these statutes and ordinances; so observe them diligently with all your heart and with all your soul.

5_26_17.NKJV: Today you have proclaimed the LORD to be your God, and that you will walk in His ways and keep His statutes, His commandments, and His judgments, and that you will obey His voice.
5_26_17.KJV: Thou hast avouched the LORD this day to be thy God, and to walk in his ways, and to keep his statutes, and his commandments, and his judgments, and to hearken unto his voice:
5_26_17.ESV: You have declared today that the LORD is your God, and that you will walk in his ways, and keep his statutes and his commandments and his rules, and will obey his voice.
5_26_17.NASB: 'You have today declared the LORD to be your God, and that you would walk in His ways and keep His statutes, His commandments and His ordinances, and listen to His voice.
5_26_17.NLT: You have declared today that the LORD is your God. And you have promised to walk in his ways, and to obey his decrees, commands, and regulations, and to do everything he tells you.
5_26_17.WEB: You have declared today that Yahweh is your God, and that you would walk in his ways, and keep his statutes, and his commandments, and his ordinances, and listen to his voice.
5_26_17.CSB17: Today you have affirmed that the LORD is your God and that you will walk in his ways, keep his statutes, commands, and ordinances, and obey him.
5_26_17.AMP: Today you have [openly] declared the LORD to be your God, and that you will walk [that is, live each and every day] in His ways and keep His statutes, His commandments, and His judgments (precepts), and listen to His voice.
5_26_17.MSG: You've renewed your vows today that God is your God, that you'll live the way he shows you; do what he tells you in the rules, regulations, and commandments; and listen obediently to him.
5_26_17.NIV: You have declared this day that the Lord is your God and that you will walk in his ways, that you will keep his decrees, commands and laws, and that you will obey him.
5_26_17.NRSVCE: Today you have obtained the LORD’s agreement: to be your God; and for you to walk in his ways, to keep his statutes, his commandments, and his ordinances, and to obey him.

5_26_18.NKJV: Also today the LORD has proclaimed you to be His special people, just as He promised you, that you should keep all His commandments,
5_26_18.KJV: And the LORD hath avouched thee this day to be his peculiar people, as he hath promised thee, and that thou shouldest keep all his commandments;
5_26_18.ESV: And the LORD has declared today that you are a people for his treasured possession, as he has promised you, and that you are to keep all his commandments,
5_26_18.NASB: 'The LORD has today declared you to be His people, a treasured possession, as He promised you, and that you should keep all His commandments;
5_26_18.NLT: The LORD has declared today that you are his people, his own special treasure, just as he promised, and that you must obey all his commands.
5_26_18.WEB: Yahweh has declared today that you are a people for his own possession, as he has promised you, and that you should keep all his commandments.
5_26_18.CSB17: And today the LORD has affirmed that you are his own possession as he promised you, that you are to keep all his commands,
5_26_18.AMP: Today the LORD has declared that you are His people, His treasured possession, just as He promised you, and that you are to keep all His commandments;
5_26_18.MSG: And today God has reaffirmed that you are dearly held treasure just as he promised, a people entrusted with keeping his commandments,
5_26_18.NIV: And the Lord has declared this day that you are his people, his treasured possession as he promised, and that you are to keep all his commands.
5_26_18.NRSVCE: Today the LORD has obtained your agreement: to be his treasured people, as he promised you, and to keep his commandments;

5_26_19.NKJV: and that He will set you high above all nations which He has made, in praise, in name, and in honor, and that you may be a holy people to the LORD your God, just as He has spoken.”
5_26_19.KJV: And to make thee high above all nations which he hath made, in praise, and in name, and in honour; and that thou mayest be an holy people unto the LORD thy God, as he hath spoken.
5_26_19.ESV: and that he will set you in praise and in fame and in honor high above all nations that he has made, and that you shall be a people holy to the LORD your God, as he promised.”
5_26_19.NASB: and that He will set you high above all nations which He has made, for praise, fame, and honor; and that you shall be a consecrated people to the LORD your God, as He has spoken.'
5_26_19.NLT: And if you do, he will set you high above all the other nations he has made. Then you will receive praise, honor, and renown. You will be a nation that is holy to the LORD your God, just as he promised.”
5_26_19.WEB: He will make you high above all nations that he has made, in praise, in name, and in honor; and that you may be a holy people to Yahweh your God, as he has spoken.
5_26_19.CSB17: that he will elevate you to praise, fame, and glory above all the nations he has made, and that you will be a holy people to the LORD your God as he promised.”
5_26_19.AMP: and that He will set you high above all the nations which He has made, for praise, fame, and honor: and that you shall be a holy people [set apart and consecrated] to the LORD your God, just as He has spoken."
5_26_19.MSG: a people set high above all other nations that he's made, high in praise, fame, and honor: you're a people holy to God, your God. That's what he has promised.
5_26_19.NIV: He has declared that he will set you in praise, fame and honor high above all the nations he has made and that you will be a people holy to the Lord your God, as he promised.
5_26_19.NRSVCE: for him to set you high above all nations that he has made, in praise and in fame and in honor; and for you to be a people holy to the LORD your God, as he promised.

5_27_1.NKJV: Now Moses, with the elders of Israel, commanded the people, saying: “Keep all the commandments which I command you today.
5_27_1.KJV: And Moses with the elders of Israel commanded the people, saying, Keep all the commandments which I command you this day.
5_27_1.ESV: Now Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people, saying, “Keep the whole commandment that I command you today.
5_27_1.NASB: Then Moses and the elders of Israel charged the people, saying, 'Keep all the commandments which I command you today.
5_27_1.NLT: Then Moses and the leaders of Israel gave this charge to the people: “Obey all these commands that I am giving you today.
5_27_1.WEB: Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people, saying, “Keep all the commandment which I command you today.
5_27_1.CSB17: Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people, “Keep every command I am giving you today.
5_27_1.AMP: Then Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people, saying, "Keep (remember, obey) all the commandments which I am commanding you today.
5_27_1.MSG: Moses commanded the leaders of Israel and charged the people: Keep every commandment that I command you today.
5_27_1.NIV: The Altar on Mount EbalMoses and the elders of Israel commanded the people: “Keep all these commands that I give you today.
5_27_1.NRSVCE: Then Moses and the elders of Israel charged all the people as follows: Keep the entire commandment that I am commanding you today.

5_27_2.NKJV: And it shall be, on the day when you cross over the Jordan to the land which the LORD your God is giving you, that you shall set up for yourselves large stones, and whitewash them with lime.
5_27_2.KJV: And it shall be on the day when ye shall pass over Jordan unto the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee, that thou shalt set thee up great stones, and plaister them with plaister:
5_27_2.ESV: And on the day you cross over the Jordan to the land that the LORD your God is giving you, you shall set up large stones and plaster them with plaster.
5_27_2.NASB: 'So it shall be on the day when you cross the Jordan to the land which the LORD your God gives you, that you shall set up for yourself large stones and coat them with lime
5_27_2.NLT: When you cross the Jordan River and enter the land the LORD your God is giving you, set up some large stones and coat them with plaster.
5_27_2.WEB: It shall be on the day when you shall pass over the Jordan to the land which Yahweh your God gives you, that you shall set yourself up great stones, and coat them with plaster.
5_27_2.CSB17: When you cross the Jordan into the land the LORD your God is giving you, set up large stones and cover them with plaster.
5_27_2.AMP: So it shall be on the day when you cross the Jordan to [enter] the land which the LORD your God gives you, that you shall set up for yourself large stones and coat them with plaster (lime, whitewash).
5_27_2.MSG: On the day you cross the Jordan into the land that God, your God, is giving you, erect large stones and coat them with plaster.
5_27_2.NIV: When you have crossed the Jordan into the land the Lord your God is giving you, set up some large stones and coat them with plaster.
5_27_2.NRSVCE: On the day that you cross over the Jordan into the land that the LORD your God is giving you, you shall set up large stones and cover them with plaster.

5_27_3.NKJV: You shall write on them all the words of this law, when you have crossed over, that you may enter the land which the LORD your God is giving you, ‘a land flowing with milk and honey,’ just as the LORD God of your fathers promised you.
5_27_3.KJV: And thou shalt write upon them all the words of this law, when thou art passed over, that thou mayest go in unto the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee, a land that floweth with milk and honey; as the LORD God of thy fathers hath promised thee.

5_27_3.NASB: and write on them all the words of this law, when you cross over, so that you may enter the land which the LORD your God gives you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, promised you.
5_27_3.NLT: Write this whole body of instruction on them when you cross the river to enter the land the LORD your God is giving you — a land flowing with milk and honey, just as the LORD, the God of your ancestors, promised you.
5_27_3.WEB: You shall write on them all the words of this law, when you have passed over; that you may go in to the land which Yahweh your God gives you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as Yahweh, the God of your fathers, has promised you.
5_27_3.CSB17: Write all the words of this law on the stones after you cross to enter the land the LORD your God is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has promised you.

5_27_3.MSG: As soon as you cross over the river, write on the stones all the words of this Revelation so that you'll enter the land that God, your God, is giving you, that land flowing with milk and honey that God, the God-of-Your-Fathers, promised you.
5_27_3.NIV: Write on them all the words of this law when you have crossed over to enter the land the Lord your God is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey, just as the Lord, the God of your fathers, promised you.

5_27_4.NKJV: Therefore it shall be, when you have crossed over the Jordan, that on Mount Ebal you shall set up these stones, which I command you today, and you shall whitewash them with lime.
5_27_4.KJV: Therefore it shall be when ye be gone over Jordan, that ye shall set up these stones, which I command you this day, in mount Ebal, and thou shalt plaister them with plaister.

5_27_4.NASB: 'So it shall be when you cross the Jordan, you shall set up on Mount Ebal, these stones, as I am commanding you today, and you shall coat them with lime.
5_27_4.NLT: When you cross the Jordan, set up these stones at Mount Ebal and coat them with plaster, as I am commanding you today.
5_27_4.WEB: It shall be, when you have crossed over the Jordan, that you shall set up these stones, which I command you today, on Mount Ebal, and you shall coat them with plaster.
5_27_4.CSB17: When you have crossed the Jordan, you are to set up these stones on Mount Ebal, as I am commanding you today, and you are to cover them with plaster.
5_27_4.AMP: Now when you cross the Jordan you shall set up these stones on Mount Ebal, just as I am commanding you today and coat them with plaster.
5_27_4.MSG: So when you've crossed the Jordan, erect these stones on Mount Ebal. Then coat them with plaster.
5_27_4.NIV: And when you have crossed the Jordan, set up these stones on Mount Ebal, as I command you today, and coat them with plaster.
5_27_4.NRSVCE: So when you have crossed over the Jordan, you shall set up these stones, about which I am commanding you today, on Mount Ebal, and you shall cover them with plaster.

5_27_5.NKJV: And there you shall build an altar to the LORD your God, an altar of stones; you shall not use an iron tool on them.

5_27_5.NASB: 'Moreover, you shall build there an altar to the LORD your God, an altar of stones; you shall not wield an iron [tool] on them.
5_27_5.NLT: “Then build an altar there to the LORD your God, using natural, uncut stones. You must not shape the stones with an iron tool.
5_27_5.WEB: There you shall build an altar to Yahweh your God, an altar of stones. You shall not use any iron tool on them.
5_27_5.CSB17: Build an altar of stones there to the LORD your God — do not use any iron tool on them.
5_27_5.AMP: There you shall build an altar to the LORD your God, an altar of stones; you shall not use an iron tool on them.
5_27_5.MSG: Build an Altar of stones for God, your God, there on the mountain. Don't use an iron tool on the stones;
5_27_5.NIV: Build there an altar to the Lord your God, an altar of stones. Do not use any iron tool upon them.
5_27_5.NRSVCE: And you shall build an altar there to the LORD your God, an altar of stones on which you have not used an iron tool.

5_27_6.NASB: 'You shall build the altar of the LORD your God of uncut stones, and you shall offer on it burnt offerings to the LORD your God;

5_27_6.WEB: You shall build Yahweh your God’s altar of uncut stones. You shall offer burnt offerings on it to Yahweh your God.
5_27_6.CSB17: Use uncut stones to build the altar of the LORD your God and offer burnt offerings to the LORD your God on it.

5_27_6.NRSVCE: You must build the altar of the LORD your God of unhewn stones. Then offer up burnt offerings on it to the LORD your God,

5_28_1.NKJV: “Now it shall come to pass, if you diligently obey the voice of the LORD your God, to observe carefully all His commandments which I command you today, that the LORD your God will set you high above all nations of the earth.
